### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2009-07-24
Tiny bugfix in the weather_script.sh file that caused super hot days to be presented by the rainy day symbol.
It seems that I had misread the weatherfont symbol for hot weather (5) with the symbol for rain (s). 
So, if you saw rain in your conky and it turned out to be something like 45 C without any spot of moisture, it's not Accuweather's fault - it's mine!!
Just replace the old file with the new one.
Sorry for any inconvenience caused...

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2009-08-23
The rss command couldn't temporarily get any answer from accuweather.
This happens whenever the site is too busy or whenever your connection is too busy (i.e. torrents, mail, heavy surfing, etc.).
Don't worry, it will come after a while.

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2009-08-26
Minor update in the wunderground-script.sh file.
Apparently, Weather Underground has a weather message (Thunderstorm) which doesn't appear in their wunderwiki site
([http://wiki.wunderground.com/index.php/API_-_XML]("http://wiki.wunderground.com/index.php/API_-_XML") - see forecast condition phrases).
As a result, whenever the forecast showed Thunderstorm as a message, you ended up with a N/A symbol.
Corrected now, just replace the old wunderground-script.sh file with the new one.

### Post by caiooiac on 2009-09-17
Hello, me again.

Just wanna ask three things: 1- What's the difference between if14 and if12 ?

2- I live in Brazil and I don't know where you live, but I know we live in different times because it changed the day 3 or 4 hours before it should have. How can I change it?

3- As I said, I'm from Brazil. Is there a way to change the messages languages?

And just one more- Is there a way to offset the whole cunky?

---

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2009-09-18
1)Wunderground forecast has 3 different states of messages:
   a) Up until midnight, the forecast is for 7 days, day and night included, i.e. Today and 6 more days (14 messages).
   b) Between midnight and morning, the messages are for Tonight and the six following days (13 messages).
   c) From somewhere in the morning until somewhere before noon, the messages are for Today and 5 more days (12 messages). The sixth day is added later on.
So, in order not to have empty conky entries I had to distinguish the cases: I count the number of messages and display them appropriately. 

2)You can't (as far as I know). I leave in Greece and my forecast changes a couple of hours AFTER local midnight (this one is worse than your case, the day has changed and I still read yesterday's forecast)

3)Hmmm... Cannot be done directly I think... You could fiddle with the script and add some additional code that will replace all the messages with Brazilian transations.

About the thermometer: Have you added the conkyweather font in your fonts?
I will repeat that if you already had it, you have to replace it with the new version of post 13.

Thanks for your kind words!!!

EDIT: Could you be a bit more specific about the offset thing?

---

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2009-10-08
New and hopefully final bugfix in the weather conditions script.
This time the "Haze" phrase in a forecast caused the non-available icon to appear.
I contacted Wunderground via e-mail and finally they updated their page with the real messages - thanks Aaron Cowdin
[http://wiki.wunderground.com/index.php/API_-_XML]("http://wiki.wunderground.com/index.php/API_-_XML")
If you're already using the script, replace the old wunderground-script.sh file with the new one.
Sorry for the continuous updates, but it hasn't been my fault!!!
Keep Ubunting...

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2009-12-06
For your first question
```
sudo apt-get install curl
```
and you're done.

To align something vertically in conky, fiddle with the voffset value,
whereas to align horizontally see the alignc and alignr variables.
An example of their usage is in my WUnderground .conkyrc file.

Post us if you manage to get it right...:P

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2009-12-17
Oh, I see....
Try this
```
background yes
use_xft yes
xftfont HandelGotD:size=11
xftalpha 0.1
update_interval 1.0
total_run_times 0
own_window yes
own_window_type normal
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
double_buffer yes
minimum_size 200 5
maximum_width 200
draw_shades no
draw_outline no
draw_borders no
draw_graph_borders no
default_color white
default_shade_color red
default_outline_color green
alignment top_right
gap_x 12
gap_y 48
no_buffers yes
uppercase no
cpu_avg_samples 2
override_utf8_locale no

TEXT
${alignc -50}${font spaceship bullet:size=60} ${time %I:%M}
${font weather:size=28}z ${font} CPU: ${acpitemp}${color0} Celsius
${font StyleBats:size=18}A${font}   CPU:  ${cpu}
${font PizzaDude Bullets:size=16}J${font}   $mem / $memmax



WEATHER ${hr 2}
${execi 600 sh /home/teo/conky_weather/weather_script.sh}
${font conkyweather:size=14}${execi 600  sed -n '4p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font}   
${voffset -5}${execi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}

${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|UK|UK248|BIRMINGHAM 10 item_title 1}
${font conkyweather:size=14}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font}  
${voffset -5}${execi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1| fold -w20}

${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=0&locCode=EUR|UK|UK248|BIRMINGHAM 10 item_title 2}
${font conkyweather:size=14}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font}  
${voffset -5}${execi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1| fold -w20}
```
Play with the value in the fold variable.

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2009-12-26
Sorry for the delay Donna. 
Of course you can...Just add to your .conkyrc file (weather section) the following cut commands:
```
...
${color Cyan}KASTORIA WEATHER ${hr 2}$color${execi 600 sh /home/teo/conky_weather/weather_script.sh}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '4p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}


${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|GR|GR022|KASTORIA 10 item_title 1}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1[COLOR="Red"]|cut -c -20[/COLOR]}


${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|GR|GR022|KASTORIA 10 item_title 2}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1[COLOR="Red"]|cut -c -20[/COLOR]}
...
```

I hope that that's what you wanted

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2010-01-22
> **lifestream said:**
> 
Is there a way to change it to:


Of course there is. In your .conky.rc file, replace these lines
```
${color Cyan}KASTORIA WEATHER ${hr 2}$color${execi 600 sh /home/teo/conky_weather/weather_script.sh}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '4p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}


[COLOR="Red"]${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|GR|GR022|KASTORIA 10 item_title 1}[/COLOR]
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}

[COLOR="Red"]${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|GR|GR022|KASTORIA 10 item_title 2}[/COLOR]
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}
```


with these

```
${color Cyan}KASTORIA WEATHER ${hr 2}$color${execi 600 sh /home/teo/conky_weather/weather_script.sh}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '4p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}


[COLOR="Green"]Today's Forecast[/COLOR]
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}


[COLOR="Green"]Tomorrow's Forecast[/COLOR]
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}
```

### Post by lifestream on 2010-01-24
Teo, thank you very much for your help :)

I learned a lot trying to fit the script to my needs, as well as the other scripts on conky. It was time well spent :) 

Now I share the conky for those interested:
```
# COLOR THEME __ Uncomment the color1 and color2 you want to use

# COLOR THEME  - PINK 
color1 8F0030 # Highlighted Values
color2 300322  # horizontal bars

# COLOR THEME  -  BLUE 
#color1 2EBAE0  # Highlighted Values
#color2 115577  # horizontal bars

# COLOR THEME  - GREEN
#color1 33AA00 # Highlighted Values
#color2 225522  # horizontal bars




# Use Xft?
use_xft yes
xftfont URW Gothic L:size=9
xftalpha 0.8
text_buffer_size 2048

# Update interval in seconds
update_interval 5

# This is the number of times Conky will update before quitting.
# Set to zero to run forever.
total_run_times 0

# Create own window instead of using desktop (required in nautilus)
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_type override
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager

# Use double buffering (reduces flicker, may not work for everyone)
double_buffer yes

# Minimum size of text area
minimum_size 180 0
#maximum_width 200

# Draw shades?
draw_shades no

# Draw outlines?
draw_outline no

# Draw borders around text
draw_borders no

# Stippled borders?
stippled_borders 0

# border margins
border_margin 5

# border width
border_width 1

# Default colors and also border colors
default_color EFE8D2
#default_shade_color black
#default_outline_color white
own_window_colour EFE8D2

# Text alignment, other possible values are commented
#alignment top_left
alignment top_right
#alignment bottom_left
#alignment bottom_right

# Gap between borders of screen and text
# same thing as passing -x at command line
gap_x 15
gap_y 15

# Subtract file system buffers from used memory?
no_buffers yes

# set to yes if you want all text to be in uppercase
uppercase no

# number of cpu samples to average
# set to 1 to disable averaging
cpu_avg_samples 1

# number of net samples to average
# set to 1 to disable averaging
net_avg_samples 2

# Force UTF8? note that UTF8 support required XFT
override_utf8_locale yes

# Add spaces to keep things from moving about?  This only affects certain objects.
use_spacer yes

#If you want to display mpd info, set the host and port
mpd_host    localhost
mpd_port    8585


TEXT

${alignc  30}${font MS Mincho:bold:size=22}${color1}&#20809;&#32654;&#38634;${font}${color}
${color2}${hr 1}${color}
CPU 1${alignr}${cpu cpu1}%  ${color2}${cpubar cpu1 8,60}${color}
CPU 2${alignr}${cpu cpu2}%  ${color2}${cpubar cpu2 8,60}${color}
RAM${alignr}${memperc}%  ${color2}${membar 8,60}${color}

${voffset 5}${color1}NAME: $alignr PID    CPU${color}
${top name 1} $alignr ${top pid 1} ${top cpu 1}
${top name 2} $alignr ${top pid 2} ${top cpu 2}
${top name 3} $alignr ${top pid 3} ${top cpu 3}

${color2}${hr 1}${color}
${alignc 30}${font URW Gothic L:size=22}${color1}${time %I:%M}${font}${color}
${alignc 15}${font URW Gothic L:size=11}${time %a  %d %b  %Y}${font}
${color2}${hr 1}${color}${font Droid Sans Mono:size=9}
${execpi 60 DJS=`date +%_d`; cal | sed '1d' | sed '/./!d' | sed 's/$/                     /' | fold -w 21 | sed -n '/^.\{21\}/p' | sed 's/^/${alignc} /' | sed /" $DJS "/s/" $DJS "/" "'${color1}'"$DJS"'${color}'" "/}
${if_up wlan0}
${color2}${hr 1}${color}
${execi 600 sh ~/.conky/conky_weather/weather_script.sh}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${color1}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' ~/.conky/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -30}${color1}Today:${color}
${offset 50}${execi 600 sed -n '2p' ~/.conky/conky_weather/weather1| fold -w20}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${color1}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' ~/.conky/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -30}${color1}Tomorrow:${color}
${offset 50}${execi 600 sed -n '3p' ~/.conky/conky_weather/weather1| fold -w21}
${endif}
${color2}${hr 1}${color}
$mpd_title 
${color1}$mpd_artist${color}

${color2}${hr 1}${color}
${color1}TODO:${color}
${execi 30 tail -n 4 -q --sleep-interval=30 ~/TODO | fold -w40 }
${color2}${hr 1}${color}

```

### Post by davec64 on 2010-02-01
Hi Teo,

Absolutely cracking scripts!
Thanks for giving me something that was easy to set up and had my location. :)

Just to follow up on a post from a few pages earlier regarding text wrapping:
```
fold -w20
```

I've had a bi of a play with the fold command, and if you throw a -s switch in the mix this will cause the fold to occur at spaces. So something like this works:

```
fold -s -w20
```

a combination of this and an $alignr (for the text) spaces it out quite nicely!

so for anybody interested here's my weather section:

```
${color Cyan}WALKHAMPTON WEATHER ${hr 2}$color${execi 600 sh /home/dave/conky_weather/weather_script.sh}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '4p' /home/dave/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}$alignr ${execi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/dave/conky_weather/weather1| fold -w30}

${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|UK|UK132|Walkhampton 10 item_title 1}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/dave/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}$alignr ${execi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/dave/conky_weather/weather1| fold -s -w30}

${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|UK|UK132|Walkhampton 10 item_title 2}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/dave/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}$alignr ${execi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/dave/conky_weather/weather1| fold -s -w30}
```

---

### Post by davec64 on 2010-02-02
It's addictive this!

Following up on from a post asking for the date in dd/mm/yyyy format, I thought I'd try and sort this as I live in the UK and that's the format we use.

It actually wasn't too bad although I would have liked to have done it using $time, but here's my solution:

Replace the Title Line 1/2 with:

```
${execi 600 date --date="-**X** days ago" '+%d/%m/%Y'} Forecast
```

Replace the "**X**" with 1 for tomorrow or 2 for the day after tomorrow.

Hope that helps!

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2010-03-04
To Finlost:
I wish I had the time to do it for you, but...

You have to use the grep command to isolate parts of the xml file that you need. For example, you can isolate the icon links (have grep filter the <icon-link> string) or the conditions' messages (grep filtering the lines with <text> in them).
Save every part of the xml file that you need in separate files. Using the sed command, you can replace abundant characters in these files to be deleted or separated from the useful staff.
When you have reached the state of the files that you want, have the .conkyrc file execute the script and then have sed read the necessary lines from the files you created in the beginning.
Read my scripts, for example, to see different techniques of isolating the important information from the rest of the weather reports.

Hope that helps....

---

Where exactly do I put this code to get dd/mm/yyyyyy format?

${execi 600 date --date="-X days ago" '+%d/%m/%Y'} Forecast

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2010-03-08
> **ancleessen4 said:**
> Where exactly do I put this code to get dd/mm/yyyyyy format?

```
${execi 600 date --date="-X days ago" '+%d/%m/%Y'} Forecast
```
```
${color Cyan}KASTORIA WEATHER ${hr 2}$color${execi 600 sh /home/teo/conky_weather/weather_script.sh}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '4p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}


${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|GR|GR022|KASTORIA 10 item_title 1}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}


${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|GR|GR022|KASTORIA 10 item_title 2}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}
```
Will become
```
${color Cyan}KASTORIA WEATHER ${hr 2}$color${execi 600 sh /home/teo/conky_weather/weather_script.sh}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '4p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}


[COLOR="Red"]${execi 600 date --date="-1 days ago" '+%d/%m/%Y'} Forecast[/COLOR]
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}


[COLOR="#ff0000"]${execi 600 date --date="-2 days ago" '+%d/%m/%Y'} Forecast[/COLOR]
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}
```


Instead of letting accuweather post the dates on whatever format it wants, we force the dates in our desired format.

This is my weather_script.sh

```

#!/bin/bash
curl "http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|CS|YI003|NOVI SAD">/home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/
sed -i 's/&/\n/g' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather
sed -i 's/>/\n/g' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather
sed -i 's/\//\n/g' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather
sed -i 's/</\n/g' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather
egrep -i 'high:|Currently:' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather>/home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1
grep -i _31x31.gif /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather>>/home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1
sed -i 's/_/\n/g' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1
sed -i "/.gif/d" /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1
a=`sed -n '4p' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1`
if test "$a" = "01"
then sed -i '4s/01/a/g' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1

For starters, change this line
```
${color Cyan}Novi Sad ${hr 2}$color${execi 600 sh /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/weather_script.sh}
```
to
```
${color Cyan}Novi Sad ${hr 2}$color${execi 600 sh /home/ech0/.conky/scripts[COLOR="Red"]/conky_weather[/COLOR]/weather_script.sh}
```
and post us the results.

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2010-09-12
Weather_script.sh, first line:
```
curl "http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|CS|YI003|NOVI SAD">/home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/
```
make it
```
curl "http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|CS|YI003|NOVI SAD">/home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather[COLOR="Red"]/weather[/COLOR]
```

For the rss, try putting your address in quotes, ie:
```
rss "http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|CS|YI003|NOVI SAD 10 item_title 1"
```

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2010-09-12
> .conkyrc

```

TEXT
${color Cyan}Novi Sad ${hr 2}$color${execi 600 sh /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather_script.sh}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '4p' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1}


${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|CS|YI003|NOVI SAD 10 item_title 1}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1}


${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|CS|YI003|NOVI SAD 10 item_title 2}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1
```

I didnt changed anything in weather_script.sh

Make these changes

```

TEXT
${color Cyan}Novi Sad ${hr 2}$color${execi 600 sh /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather_script.sh}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '4p' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1}${font} [COLOR="red"]${alignr}[/COLOR]${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1[COLOR="red"]|fold -w30[/COLOR]}


${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|CS|YI003|NOVI[COLOR="Red"]_[/COLOR]SAD 10 item_title 1}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1}${font} [COLOR="red"]${alignr}[/COLOR]${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1[COLOR="red"]|fold -w30[/COLOR]}


${rss http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|CS|YI003|NOVI[COLOR="red"]_[/COLOR]SAD 10 item_title 2}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1}${font} [COLOR="red"]${alignr}[/COLOR]${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/ech0/.conky/scripts/conky_weather/weather1[COLOR="red"]|fold -w30[/COLOR]}
```

Play with the values in the fold part to get it right.
Don't overlook the underscore in the NOVI_SAD part in the rss command.

### Post by MiKOTRON on 2011-01-15
Is there something that can be done about the word wrap problem, where the wrapped text is covered by the icons?

Here's my .conkyrc
```
background yes
use_xft yes
xftfont HandelGotD:size=9
xftalpha 0.5
update_interval 1.0
total_run_times 0
own_window yes
own_window_type normal
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
double_buffer yes
minimum_size 250 5
maximum_width 250
draw_shades no
draw_outline no
draw_borders no
draw_graph_borders no
default_color white
default_shade_color red
default_outline_color green
alignment top_right
gap_x 12
gap_y 48
no_buffers yes
uppercase no
cpu_avg_samples 2
override_utf8_locale no

TEXT
${color orange}SYSTEM ${hr 2} $color
$sysname $kernel $alignr $machine
Uptime $alignr $uptime
Load $alignr $loadavg
$processes processes ($running_processes running)

NAME${alignr}PID         CPU%        MEM%
${top name 1}${alignr}${top pid 1}       ${top cpu 1}          ${top mem 1}   
${top name 2}${alignr}${top pid 2}       ${top cpu 2}          ${top mem 2}   
${top name 3}${alignr}${top pid 3}       ${top cpu 3}          ${top mem 3}   
${top name 4}${alignr}${top pid 4}       ${top cpu 4}          ${top mem 4}

${color orange}Ethernet (${addr eth0}) ${hr 2} $color
Down: $color${downspeed eth0} k/s ${alignr}Up: ${upspeed eth0} k/s
${downspeedgraph eth0 20,120 e5e5e5 F1AA0E} ${alignr}${upspeedgraph eth0
20,120 e5e5e5 F1AA0E}$color
Total: ${totaldown eth0} ${alignr}Total: ${totalup eth0}
Inbound: ${tcp_portmon 1 32767 count} Outbound: ${tcp_portmon 32768
61000 count}${alignr}Total: ${tcp_portmon 1 65535 count}

${color orange}CPU ${hr 2} $color
${freq}MHz${alignr}Load: ${loadavg}
${alignr}${loadgraph 20,250 e5e5e5 F1AA0E}
CPU Total:${color} ${cpu cpu0}% ${color}${alignr}Temp:${color} ${execi 2 cat /sys/bus/pci/drivers/k8temp/000*/temp1_input | cut -c1,2 | awk '{print ($1*9)/5+32}'}${iconv_start UTF-8 ISO_8859-1}°F ${iconv_stop}
${alignr}${cpugraph 0 20,250 e5e5e5 F1AA0E}

${color orange}Memory ${hr 2} $color
RAM $mem / $memmax ${alignr}${membar 8,60}
Swap $swap / $swapmax ${alignr}${swapbar 8,60}

${color orange}HDD ${hr 2} $color
/ $alignc ${fs_used /} / ${fs_size /} $alignr ${fs_free_perc /}%
${fs_bar /}

/home $alignc ${fs_used /home} / ${fs_size /home} $alignr ${fs_free_perc /home}%
${fs_bar /home}

${color orange}Experimental! ${hr 2} $color
${color Cyan}WEATHER ${hr 2}$color${execi 600 sh /home/mike/conky_weather/weather_script.sh}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '4p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${alignr}${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1}

${execi 600 date --date="-1 days ago" '+%m/%d/%Y'} Forecast
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${alignr}${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1| fold -w30}

${execi 600 date --date="-2 days ago" '+%m/%d/%Y'} Forecast
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${alignr}${voffset -20}${execi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1| fold -w30}

```I've also attached a screenshot.  Thanks for your time.

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-01-16
Change all instances of execi to execpi
```
${color orange}Experimental! ${hr 2} $color
${color Cyan}WEATHER ${hr 2}$color${execpi 600 sh /home/mike/conky_weather/weather_script.sh}
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execpi 600  sed -n '4p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${alignr}${voffset -20}${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather1}

${execpi 600 date --date="-1 days ago" '+%m/%d/%Y'} Forecast
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execpi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${alignr}${voffset -20}${execpi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1| fold -w30}

${execpi 600 date --date="-2 days ago" '+%m/%d/%Y'} Forecast
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execpi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${alignr}${voffset -20}${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1| fold -w30}
```

Execp parses text much better than exec.

/ $alignc ${fs_used /} / ${fs_size /} $alignr ${fs_free_perc /}%
${fs_bar /}

/home $alignc ${fs_used /home} / ${fs_size /home} $alignr ${fs_free_perc /home}%
${fs_bar /home}

${color orange}Weather ${hr 2} $color ${execpi 600 sh /home/mike/conky_weather/weather_script.sh}
Today's Conditions
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execpi 600  sed -n '4p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1}${font}   ${voffset -20}${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1}


${execpi 600 date --date="-1 days ago" '+%m/%d'}'s Forecast
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execpi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${alignr}${execpi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1| fold -w30}

${execpi 600 date --date="-2 days ago" '+%m/%d'}'s Forecast
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execpi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1}${font} ${alignr}${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/mike/conky_weather/weather1| fold -w30}



```

### Post by gauravj on 2011-02-15
Hi

Here are python files to display the accuweather info in conky.
The weather script parses data and also **downloads weather images / icons to local file system. it improves images by removing background programatically with the help of other python script, the list of images processed is stored with the help of python pickle module.**
The setup is quite simple find the city id OR country, city and state as in url of accuweather and give it as a command line argument to weather script in conky config.

Will be little tough for newbees, so newbees please help yourself.

It is easy, just a python script is used to parse the data and the python program accepts the arguments from command line.

**WEATHER ON CONSOLE**
There is a print console method in script, please modify it to your need, to display weather information on your terminal.

**OTHER USAGES**
The script extracts data based on id of element, so pages which have id attribute in html elements, can be parsed for tag value and attribute value.

### Post by Wolle69 on 2011-02-24
Hi Teo,

I tried hard to find words for this, but ended up rather lyrical each time. To put it in a nutshell: I'm really thankful for your work, providing this script. I was in search of a conky weather script, that doesn't need any registration anywhere, so the weather underground script seemed to be perfect.

Well... then I tried to understand the scripts and decided to fit them to my needs. In the end, I produced my own package, consisting of a .conkyrc, weather underground script, user settings file for that script and a locale file, that can easily be adopted to ones needs. For the sake of completeness I include the fonts as well.

My scripts contain some pieces of your code, but were mostly (~85-90%?) rewritten, as I prefer a more structured way (using for-loops, case instead of if) for both programming and data (stored each kind of information in a seperate file). Well... I'm still a beginner, regarding bash programming and one could write that even better for sure - who knows? Still, I had to learn a lot concerning especially the sed utility and bash programming... your scripts and the work with them were a nice piece of lesson.

Please don't feel offended, by me not only hijacking your thread, but also your scripts. But I believe, that this is the way of open source... so I'll provide my package here. Feel free to comment, rant or do whatever you like.

Cheers and kudos again to you!
    Wolle

---

### Post by Wolle69 on 2011-02-24
Conky Weather Underground Localized

Features:

[LIST]
[*]uses combined symbols for wind direction and speed, omits literal direction/speed information
[*]adds chance of precipitation notice
[*]re-assigned the weather symbols for some weather messages
[*]localized (you can create your own locales file)
[*]easy to adopt: Just edit the config file for your language and the ical link, leave the script untouched. Layout of the conkyrc is defined with templates, which you can easily alter.
[*]some changes "under the hood"... ;)
[/LIST]
 
Usage:

[LIST=1]
[*]Download package and unpack.
[*]Store the fonts of ./fonts in your ~/.fonts directory. If that doesn't exist, simply create it.
[*] Store .conky_wugl_rc in your ~/.conky directory.
[*]Also store conky_wunderground_localized directory in ~/.conky. (It  should be ~/.conky/conky_wunderground_localized then... anyway, it  shouldn't really matter).
[*] Edit .conky_wugl_rc:
[LIST=1]
[*]Search for the templates section.
[*] Edit template0 to the correct path, where the  conky_wunderground_localized.sh script resides. Keep the "\1" at the end  of the path as indicated in the example! You better only change the  username, assuming that you used the paths I recommended above.
[*]Edit template1-9 according to your needs. I tried to comment  everything in a comprehensive way. You may skip this point if you don't  want to make any cosmetic changes...
[/LIST]
 
[*] Edit conky_wunderground_localized_rc
[LIST=1]
[*]Set the correct ical link for your place.
[*]Set your preferred locale setting.
[*]If no locales file for your language does exist yet, copy the one,  that's in the package and edit it for your language, as you like it.
[/LIST]
 
[*]Make the conky_wunderground_localized.sh script executable, for example like this:
```
 ~$ chmod +x ./.conky/conky_wunderground_localized/conky_wunderground_localized.sh
```
[*]Set up your conky to use the .conky_wugl_rc
[/LIST]
That's it. Enjoy and do whatever you like with the scripts...

Screenshot:
[IMG]http://img683.imageshack.us/i/bildschirmfotof.png/[/IMG][[IMG]http://img683.imageshack.us/img683/8594/bildschirmfotof.png[/IMG]]("http://img683.imageshack.us/i/bildschirmfotof.png/")

Uploaded with [ImageShack.us]("http://imageshack.us")

Cheers
    Wolle

---

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-04-13
> **techno-mole said:**
> Me again.

Just a question about the icons really, it seems this morning some of the icons for both the weather underground and accuweather forecasts aren't loading, the clouds and such like, the temp icon is there though.

Is this something to do with the respective providers ? both are giving a forecast, just no cloud icons.

I did double check the font files and such like, but if they were working yesterday they should be working to do as I haven't made any changes, would I be right in thinking the icons are acquired from the weather provider ?

I did try running each conky config on it's own to see if it was some kind of conflict but it didn't make any difference.

I attached a screenshot so you can see what I mean.

Many thanks.

No the icons are provided by the conkyweather font, according to what the forecast is - that's what the script does: it translates the forecasts into letters corresponding to the appropriate symbols of the font.

Stop conky
```
killall conky
```
Delete everything (all temp files) from your conky_weather folder apart from the weather_script.sh script, and everything from your conky_wunderground folder apart from wunderground-script.sh.
Restart conky: Alt+F2 and give
```
conky
```
Post back with the outcome.

conkyrc2 ---> (this has the weather underground stuff)

```
# UBUNTU-CONKY

# Create own window instead of using desktop (required in nautilus)
own_window yes
own_window_type override
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager

# Use double buffering (reduces flicker, may not work for everyone)
double_buffer yes

# fiddle with window
use_spacer yes
use_xft yes

# Update interval in seconds
update_interval 3.0

# Minimum size of text area
minimum_size 750 5
maximum_height 750


override_utf8_locale yes

# Draw shades?
draw_shades yes

# Text stuff
draw_outline yes # amplifies text if yes
draw_borders no
#font FreeSans -10
xftfont freesans:pixelsize=11
uppercase no # set to yes if you want all text to be in uppercase

# Stippled borders?
stippled_borders 3

# border margins
border_margin 9

# border width
border_width 10

# Default colors and also border colors, grey90 == #e5e5e5
default_color EBCA92

own_window_colour brown
own_window_transparent yes

# Text alignment, other possible values are commented
alignment top_left
#alignment top_right
#alignment bottom_left
#alignment bottom_right

# Gap between borders of screen and text
gap_x 350
gap_y 40

# stuff after 'TEXT' will be formatted on screen

TEXT
${execi 600 sh /home/techno-mole/conky_wunderground/wunderground-script.sh}${if_existing /home/techno-mole/conky_wunderground/14}${color 7D9996}Today ${hr 2}$color        
${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '15p' /home/techno-mole/conky_wunderground/icons.wun}${font} ${voffset -22}${execi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/techno-mole/conky_wunderground/messages.wun}   
${alignc -10}${voffset -27}${font conkyweather:size=35}E${font}${voffset -20}${offset -10}${execi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/techno-mole/conky_wunderground/messages.wun}
${alignr 40}${voffset -27}${font ConkyWindNESW:size=35}${execi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/techno-mole/conky_wunderground/icons.wun}${font}${voffset -15}${execi 600 sed -n '4p' /home/techno-mole/conky_wunderground/messages.wun}  

${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '16p' /home/techno-mole/conky_wunderground/icons.wun}${font} ${voffset -22}${execi 600 sed -n '6p' /home/techno-mole/conky_wunderground/messages.wun}

Here are the scripts for accuweather and weather underground.

accuweather.

```
#!/bin/bash
curl "http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|GR|GR022|KASTORIA">/home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather
sed -i 's/&/\n/g' /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather
sed -i 's/>/\n/g' /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather
sed -i 's/\//\n/g' /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather
sed -i 's/</\n/g' /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather
egrep -i 'high:|Currently:' /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather>/home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather1
grep -i _31x31.gif /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather>>/home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather1 
sed -i 's/_/\n/g' /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather1
sed -i "/.gif/d" /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather1
a=`sed -n '4p' /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather1`
if test "$a" = "01"
then sed -i '4s/01/a/g' /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather1
elif test "$a" = "02"
then sed -i '4s/02/b/g' /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather1
elif test "$a" = "03"
then sed -i '4s/03/c/g' /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather1
elif test "$a" = "04"
then sed -i '4s/04/d/g' /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather1
elif test "$a" = "05"
then sed -i '4s/05/c/g' /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather1
elif test "$a" = "06"
then sed -i '4s/06/d/g' /home/techno-mole/conky_weather/weather1

weather underground.

```
#!/bin/bash
wget -O ~/conky_wunderground/ics1 http://ical.wunderground.com/auto/ical/global/stations/03492.ics?units=metric
sed  's/\./\n/g' ~/conky_wunderground/ics1 > ~/conky_wunderground/ics
egrep -i 'description|monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday|sunday|high|low|wind' ~/conky_wunderground/ics >~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun
sed -i 's/DESCRIPTION://g' ~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun
sed -i 's/\\n/\n/g' ~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun
sed -i 's/ - /\n/g' ~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun
sed -i 's/High/\nHigh/g' ~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun
sed -i 's/Low/\nLow/g' ~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun
cp ~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun ~/conky_wunderground/temp1.wun
sed -i 's/Wind /\n/g' ~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun
sed -i '/^ /d' ~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun
sed -i '/^$/d' ~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun
sed -i 's/Wind/\nWind/g' ~/conky_wunderground/temp1.wun
sed -i 's/\./\n/g' ~/conky_wunderground/temp1.wun
grep -i Wind ~/conky_wunderground/temp1.wun>~/conky_wunderground/wind-icons-messages.wun
sed -i 's/Wind //g' ~/conky_wunderground/wind-icons-messages.wun
sed -i 's/ km\/h//g' ~/conky_wunderground/wind-icons-messages.wun
cut -c1-3 ~/conky_wunderground/wind-icons-messages.wun >~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
rm ~/conky_wunderground/14
rm ~/conky_wunderground/13
rm ~/conky_wunderground/12
a=`sed -n '$=' ~/conky_wunderground/wind-icons-messages.wun`
if  [ $a -eq "13" ];
then sed -i '1s/^/Today\n   -\n       -                \n        -                \n/' ~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun
sed -i '1s/^/\n/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
echo $a>~/conky_wunderground/13
elif [ $a -eq "12" ];
then sed -i '12s/$/\n\n/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
sed -i '48s/$/\n\n   -\n       -                \n        -                \n\n   -\n       -                \n        -                \n/' ~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun
echo $a>~/conky_wunderground/12
elif [ $a -eq "14" ];
then echo $a>~/conky_wunderground/14
fi;
a=`sed -n '2p' ~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun`
if test "$a" = "Chance of Flurries"
then sed -i '14s/$/\np/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Chance of Rain"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nh/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Chance of Freezing Rain" || test "$a" = "Light Freezing Rain" || test "$a" = "Light Freezing Rain"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nv/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Chance of Sleet"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nw/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Chance of Snow"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nq/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Chance of Thunderstorms"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nn/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Chance of a Thunderstorm"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nm/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Clear"
then sed -i '14s/$/\na/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Cloudy"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nd/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Flurries"
then sed -i '14s/$/\np/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Fog"
then sed -i '14s/$/\n0/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Haze"
then sed -i '14s/$/\n0/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Mostly Cloudy"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nc/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Mostly Sunny"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nb/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Partly Cloudy"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nc/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Partly Sunny"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nc/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Freezing Rain"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nv/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Rain"
then sed -i '14s/$/\ni/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Sleet"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nw/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Snow"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nr/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Sunny"
then sed -i '14s/$/\na/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Thunderstorms" || test "$a" = "Thunderstorm"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nn/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Overcast"
then sed -i '14s/$/\ne/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Scattered Clouds"
then sed -i '14s/$/\nb/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
else sed -i '14s/$/\n-/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
fi;

then sed -i '10s/SW /3/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "WSW"
then sed -i '10s/WSW/4/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Wes"
then sed -i '10s/Wes/5/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "WNW"
then sed -i '10s/WNW/6/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "NW "
then sed -i '10s/NW /7/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "NNW"
then sed -i '10s/NNW/8/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Nor"
then sed -i '10s/Nor/9/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "NNE"
then sed -i '10s/NNE/:/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "NE "
then sed -i '10s/NE /;/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "ENE"
then sed -i '10s/ENE/</g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "Eas"
then sed -i '10s/Eas/=/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "ESE"
then sed -i '10s/ESE/>/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "SE "
then sed -i '10s/SE /?/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
elif test "$a" = "SSE"
then sed -i '10s/SSE/@/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
else sed -i '10s/$/-/' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun
fi;
a=`sed -n '44p' ~/conky_wunderground/messages.wun|cut -c1-3 `
if test "$a" = "Sou"
then sed -i '11s/Sou/1/g' ~/conky_wunderground/icons.wun

### Post by shirazs on 2011-05-15
> **leona said:**
> Hi.

these scripts are great, like others, I didn't want to use weather.com either, (noticed lots of people having problems with registration and I didn't want to register on to another site to possibily receive emails), so this works great.
I just wonder, have the UK users, tried using the RSS feeds from the BBC with any scripts, like [http://newsrss.bbc.co.uk/weather/forecast/329/Next3DaysRSS.xml]("http://newsrss.bbc.co.uk/weather/forecast/329/Next3DaysRSS.xml"). just wondered?
Using $scroll and $rss, my .conkyrc has:

${scroll 82 3 ${rss [http://newsrss.bbc.co.uk/weather/forecast/2688/Next3DaysRSS.xml?area=NP44](http://newsrss.bbc.co.uk/weather/forecast/2688/Next3DaysRSS.xml?area=NP44) 10 feed_title} | ${rss [http://newsrss.bbc.co.uk/weather/forecast/2688/Next3DaysRSS.xml?area=NP44](http://newsrss.bbc.co.uk/weather/forecast/2688/Next3DaysRSS.xml?area=NP44) 10 item_title 0} | ${rss [http://newsrss.bbc.co.uk/weather/forecast/2688/Next3DaysRSS.xml?area=NP44](http://newsrss.bbc.co.uk/weather/forecast/2688/Next3DaysRSS.xml?area=NP44) 10 item_desc 0} | ${rss [http://newsrss.bbc.co.uk/weather/forecast/2688/Next3DaysRSS.xml?area=NP44](http://newsrss.bbc.co.uk/weather/forecast/2688/Next3DaysRSS.xml?area=NP44) 10 item_title 1} | ${rss [http://newsrss.bbc.co.uk/weather/forecast/2688/Next3DaysRSS.xml?area=NP44](http://newsrss.bbc.co.uk/weather/forecast/2688/Next3DaysRSS.xml?area=NP44) 10 item_desc 1}}

Change value after area= to suit where you are

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-06-13
Sorry mate, but there's nothing that can be done:
```
sed -i 's/\//\n/g' /home/teo/conky_weather/weather
```
This line replaces all instances of / with newline characters throughout the RSS feed - it is essential for its parsing.
I'll see what I can do and post back, but don't hold your breath...
Sorry...

---

### Post by Tr3LoS on 2011-06-13
I see.
I will be checking for feedback here.

---

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-06-13
Done!!!!!
I can't believe nobody has ever noticed it before - thanks to Tr3LoS for doing so.
The solution was as simple as to delay the sed command until egrep has gathered all the messages it needs.

Just replace the old weather_script.sh with the new one (see page 1) and you're done.

Thanks again to Tr3Los.

---

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-08-19
Change all the fold -w25 instances to fold -w35 (or even 40).
Play with this value to get the desired result.
If necessary, increase also the maximum_width value of your .conkyrc.

---

### Post by FrAggLE-Rock on 2011-08-20
here is the url:
[http://www.accuweather.com/en-us/at/vorarlberg/mittelberg/quick-look.aspx?cityid=26226](http://www.accuweather.com/en-us/at/vorarlberg/mittelberg/quick-look.aspx?cityid=26226)
and the conky

```
# Use Xft?
use_xft yes
xftfont OFL Sorts Mill Goudy:size=8
xftalpha 0.8
text_buffer_size 2048

# Update interval in seconds
update_interval 1

# This is the number of times Conky will update before quitting.
# Set to zero to run forever.
total_run_times 0

# Create own window instead of using desktop (required in nautilus)
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_type override
own_window_class conky
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
background no

# Use double buffering (reduces flicker, may not work for everyone)
double_buffer yes

# Minimum size of text area
#minimum_size 340 0 
#maximum_width 350

# Draw shades?
draw_shades no

# Draw outlines?
draw_outline no

# Draw borders around text
draw_borders no

# Stippled borders?
stippled_borders 0

# border margins
border_inner_margin 5

# border width
border_width 1

# Default colors and also border colors
#default_color white



# Text alignment, other possible values are commented
#alignment top_left
alignment top_right
#alignment bottom_left
#alignment bottom_right

# Gap between borders of screen and text
# same thing as passing -x at command line
gap_x 35
gap_y 60

# Subtract file system buffers from used memory?
no_buffers yes

# set to yes if you want all text to be in uppercase
uppercase no

# number of cpu samples to average
# set to 1 to disable averaging
cpu_avg_samples 8

# number of net samples to average
# set to 1 to disable averaging
net_avg_samples 2

# Force UTF8? note that UTF8 support required XFT
override_utf8_locale yes

# Add spaces to keep things from moving about?  This only affects certain objects.
use_spacer none

imlib_cache_size 0

color1 Ivory
color2 Ivory2
color3 Ivory3
color4 Tan1
color5 white
color6 Gray
color7 AntiqueWhite4
color8 DarkSlateGray
color9 Black
TEXT
##################
##     TIME     ##
##################
#${goto 85}${font OpenLogos:size=40}${color black} t ${goto 150}${font OpenLogos:size=40} T 
#${font Arial:bold:size=12}${color4}Time ${color8}${hr 2}${color2}
${voffset 4}${font RadioSpace:size=32}${alignc 5}${time %R}
${voffset -10}${font RadioSpace:bold:size=18}${alignc}${time %A}, ${offset 5}${time %d %b}
##################
##     System   ##
##################

${voffset -70}${font Arial:bold:size=12}${color4}System ${color8}${hr 2}${color2}${font} 

${voffset 4}${color5}${font OpenLogos:size=16}u${font Arial:bold:size=11}${color2}   ${goto 45}${voffset -4}Ubuntu:   ${alignr}${kernel}
${voffset 4}${color5}${font OpenLogos:size=16}T${font Arial:bold:size=11}${color2}   ${goto 45}${voffset -4}Uptime:  ${alignr}${uptime}
${voffset 4}${color5}${font StyleBats:size=16}A${font Arial:bold:size=11}${color2}   ${goto 45}${voffset -4}CPU:  ${goto 108}${cpu cpu}% ${alignr} ${cpugraph cpu1 13,155 000000 000000}
${voffset 4}${color5}${font StyleBats:size=16}g${font Arial:bold:size=11}${color2}   ${goto 45}${voffset -4}RAM:  ${goto 108}$memperc% ${alignr} ${memgraph 13,155 000000 000000}
${voffset 4}${color5}${font PizzaDude Bullets:size=16}D${font Arial:bold:size=11}${color2}  ${goto 45}${voffset -4}CPU Temperature: ${alignr}${hwmon temp 1}${font Arial:bold:size=11} °C
${voffset 4}${color5}${font PizzaDude Bullets:size=16}A${font Arial:bold:size=11}${color2}  ${goto 45}${voffset -4}GPU Temperature:${alignr}${font Arial:bold:size=11}${color1} ${font Arial:bold:size=11}${exec nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | grep Attribute | cut -d ' ' -f 6 | cut -c 1-2} °C
##################
##     Network  ##
##################
${voffset 10}${if_up wlan0}${font Arial:bold:size=12}${color4}Network ${color8}${hr 2}${color2}
${voffset 10}${color5}${font PizzaDude Bullets:size=14}T ${color2}${font Arial:bold:size=11}${voffset -1}Down: ${goto 100}${downspeedf wlan0} KB ${alignr}${downspeedgraph wlan0 13,150 000000 000000}
${voffset 8}${color5}${font PizzaDude Bullets:size=14}N ${color2}${font Arial:bold:size=11}${voffset -1}Up: ${goto 100}${upspeedf wlan0} KB ${alignr}${upspeedgraph wlan0 13,150 000000 000000}
${voffset 8}${color5}${font PizzaDude Bullets:size=14}Z ${color2}${font Arial:bold:size=11}SSID:  ${wireless_essid wlan0}: ${alignr}${wireless_link_qual wlan0}%
##################
##   Weather    ##
##################
${voffset 10}${if_up wlan0}${font Arial:bold:size=12}${color4}Weather ${color8}${hr 2}${color2}
${execi 600 bash /home/dominik/accuweather_conky/accuw_script}
${voffset -20}${goto 90}${font Weather:size=50}${color2}y${font Radiospace:bold:size=22}${voffset -15}${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/dominik/accuweather_conky/temperatures}° ${font Radiospace:bold:size=22}${color4}${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/dominik/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -w40}  
${color5}${goto 145}${voffset -10}${font conkyweather:size=80}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' ~/accuweather_conky/images}

${voffset -130}${color5}${font conkyweather:size=60}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' ~/accuweather_conky/images}${font Arial:bold:size=12}${goto 100}${voffset -50}${color4}${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/dominik/accuweather_conky/days}:
${color5}${goto 100}${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/dominik/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -w40}
${goto 100}High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/dominik/accuweather_conky/temperatures}°  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/dominik/accuweather_conky/temperatures}°

${font conkyweather:size=60}${execi 600  sed -n '7p' ~/accuweather_conky/images}${font Arial:bold:size=12}${goto 100}${voffset -50}${color4}${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/dominik/accuweather_conky/days}:
${color5}${goto 100}${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/dominik/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -w40}
${goto 100}High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '4p' /home/dominik/accuweather_conky/temperatures}°  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/dominik/accuweather_conky/temperatures}°

${font conkyweather:size=60}${execi 600  sed -n '8p' ~/accuweather_conky/images}${font Arial:bold:size=12}${goto 100}${voffset -50}${color4}${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/dominik/accuweather_conky/days}:
${color5}${goto 100}${execpi 600 sed -n '7p' /home/dominik/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -w40}

```
# Use Xft?
use_xft yes
xftfont OFL Sorts Mill Goudy:size=8
xftalpha 0.8
text_buffer_size 2048

# Update interval in seconds
update_interval 1

# This is the number of times Conky will update before quitting.
# Set to zero to run forever.
total_run_times 0

# Create own window instead of using desktop (required in nautilus)
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_type override
own_window_class conky
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
background no

# Use double buffering (reduces flicker, may not work for everyone)
double_buffer yes

# Minimum size of text area
minimum_size 340 0 
#maximum_width 350

# Draw shades?
draw_shades no

# Draw outlines?
draw_outline no

# Draw borders around text
draw_borders no

# Stippled borders?
stippled_borders 0

# border margins
border_inner_margin 5

# border width
border_width 1

# Default colors and also border colors
#default_color white



# Text alignment, other possible values are commented
#alignment top_left
alignment top_right
#alignment bottom_left
#alignment bottom_right

# Gap between borders of screen and text
# same thing as passing -x at command line
gap_x 35
gap_y 60

# Subtract file system buffers from used memory?
no_buffers yes

# set to yes if you want all text to be in uppercase
uppercase no

# number of cpu samples to average
# set to 1 to disable averaging
cpu_avg_samples 8

# number of net samples to average

EDIT (August 2nd, 2011): I don't know what happened to the page, but apparently it's not currently available. I've contacted Accuweather (read from post #152 onwards) but I still have to get an answer from them. Sorry for any inconvenience but it's not my fault...



Here is a list of cities. It could help some of you guys... 

```

http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=EUR|DE|GM003|BERLIN		Berlin  Germany
http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=NAM|US|NY|NEW YORK		New York US
http://rss.accuweather.com/rss/liveweather_rss.asp?metric=1&locCode=80201		Denver CO

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-10-15
Yours doesn't work.
Try with this conkyrc:
```
own_window yes
own_window_type override
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_hints undecorated,below,skip_taskbar,skip_pager

# Use double buffering (reduces flicker, may not work for everyone)
double_buffer yes

# fiddle with window
use_spacer yes
use_xft yes

# Update interval in seconds
update_interval 3.0

# Minimum size of text area
minimum_size 230 500
maximum_width 216

override_utf8_locale yes

# Draw shades?
draw_shades yes

# Text stuff
draw_outline yes # amplifies text if yes
draw_borders no
#font freesans -12
xftfont Arial:size=9
uppercase no # set to yes if you want all text to be in uppercase

# Stippled borders?
stippled_borders 3

# border margins
border_margin 9

# border width
border_width 10

# Default colors and also border colors, grey90 == #e5e5e5
default_color cbcbcb

own_window_colour brown
own_window_transparent yes

# Text alignment, other possible values are commented
alignment top_left
#alignment top_right
#alignment bottom_left
#alignment bottom_right

# Gap between borders of screen and text
gap_x 10
gap_y 30

# stuff after 'TEXT' will be formatted on screen

TEXT
${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' ~/accuweather_conky_USA/images}${font}${goto 75}${voffset -40}${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/days}:
${goto 75}${execpi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/messages|fold -w25}
${goto 75}High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures}F  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures}F

${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '7p' ~/accuweather_conky_USA/images}${font}${goto 75}${voffset -40}${execpi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/days}:
${goto 75}${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/messages|fold -w25}
${goto 75}High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures}F  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '4p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures}F

${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '8p' ~/accuweather_conky_USA/images}${font}${goto 75}${voffset -40}${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/days}:
${goto 75}${execpi 600 sed -n '8p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/messages|fold -w25}
${goto 75}High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures}F  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '6p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures}F

${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '9p' ~/accuweather_conky_USA/images}${font}${goto 75}${voffset -40}${execpi 600 sed -n '4p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/days}:
${goto 75}${execpi 600 sed -n '11p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/messages|fold -w25}
${goto 75}High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '7p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures}F  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '8p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures}F

${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '10p' ~/accuweather_conky_USA/images}${font}${goto 75}${voffset -40}${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/days}:
${goto 75}${execpi 600 sed -n '14p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/messages|fold -w25}
${goto 75}High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '9p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures}F  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '10p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures}F
```
It works for me.

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-10-15
Ahh, I see. 
Sorry mate, but the USA script doesn't supply the current temp.
The one FragglErock uses is the international script, which does have an entry for it.

As for the display position, change this
```
# Text alignment, other possible values are commented
alignment top_left
#alignment top_right
#alignment bottom_left
#alignment bottom_right
```
to this
```
# Text alignment, other possible values are commented
#alignment top_left
alignment top_right
#alignment bottom_left
#alignment bottom_right
```

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-10-15
You can't get info for a place in the USA with the international script; you have to use the USA script.
The reason for this is that the pages in the case of a US location and an international location are totally different (racist American imperialists :lolflag:), therefore two different scripts had to be made.
What do you need the current temp anyway?
I'd prefer to have one more day in my forecast, than to have the current temp in it, something that I could find just opening my window.

Hey Teo ....

REF: 2)WUnderground ICAL

When I run the conky "today" doesn't show up.

In fact it starts with tomorrow.

Another change I made to eliminate this:

Find the
```
/home/teo/
```
string and replace it with
```
/home/yourusername/
```
Save and exit.

I did this:
```
/home/teo/
```
string and replace it with
```
$HOME/
```

conkyrc2 now good anywhere.  :D

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-10-21
@General_Faliure: Thanks for the kind words.

@ichase: The update interval is dependent on accuweather. I run the USA script and it is indeed showing Friday as tomorrow - I think it's normal.
I'll leave it on all day today and see about the updating thing; although, as I said, the update interval is a matter of accuweather.
If you want to test the accuracy of the forecast, all you have to do is open the accuweather page whose address you copied in the script and compare it with conky (see attached screenshot).

@Sector11: Thanks for the tip and you're absolutely right, no today on the forecast. From a quick look I discovered that wunderground has changed the layout of their forecast page and a rewrite of the script is due. I will notify you via pm when the new version is ready - be patient, the script has to be on watch for about 24h.

Well it seems to be a caching issue in regards to my weather. After initially setting it up on Wednesday, it received the data and posted the data via my conky. This is on both the desktop and laptop. Since the initial data came in that was all that was received. Nothing else I can think of for this to be happening.
 
I even went as far as deleting my .conkyrc and starting from scratch with just the weather data. I did not use existing code but code that I got in this thread. The original data that I received from the first time I set it up on Wednesday. So I am going to hold off on that weather for a while.

egrep -i 'lunes|martes|miercoles|jueves|viernes|sábado|domingo' /home/teo/Desktop/raw > /home/teo/Desktop/days
```Notice the little thingy above sabado and also that it's domingo and not domin go.

Teo thanks a lots!!!!... my conky weather is Ok now!

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-10-22
HA!!!! I knew it!!!
Accuweather changed its layout, Weather Underground changed its layout, so why would the National Weather Service stay behind: they changed their layout as well. So...

**_UPDATE:_**
NWS package rebundled as the NWS changed its XML forecast.

Starting with: **1a)Accuweather - INTERNATIONAL USERS**
I have my first Teo weather conky.

[CENTER][[IMG]http://thumbnails44.imagebam.com/15525/7951bf155240482.jpg[/IMG]](http://www.imagebam.com/image/7951bf155240482)[/CENTER]

No good without the conky:
```
## killall conky && conky -c ~/TBG/ACC/.conkyrc

 ###  Begin Window Settings  ##################################################
 # Create own window instead of using desktop (required in nautilus)
own_window yes
own_window_type override
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_hints below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
# own_window_colour brown
own_window_class Conky
own_window_title AccuWeather Bs As

 ### ARGB can be used for real transparency
 ### NOTE that a composite manager is required for real transparency.
 ### This option will not work as desired (in most cases) in conjunction with
 ### 'own_window_type override'
# own_window_argb_visual yes
 ### When ARGB visuals are enabled, this use this to modify the alpha value
 ### Valid range is 0-255, where 0 is 0% opacity, and 255 is 100% opacity. 
#own_window_argb_value 0

minimum_size 150 0	## width, height
maximum_width 150	## width - usually a good idea to equal minimum width.

gap_x 10	## left &right
gap_y 10	## up & down

alignment top_right
 ####################################################  End Window Settings  ###
 ###  Font Settings  ##########################################################
 # Use Xft (anti-aliased font and stuff) 
use_xft yes
xftfont monospace:size=8
 # Alpha of Xft font. Must be a value at or between 1 and 0 ###
xftalpha 0
 # Force UTF8? requires XFT ###
override_utf8_locale yes

draw_shades no
draw_outline no
uppercase no

draw_outline no # amplifies text if yes
 ######################################################  End Font Settings  ###
 ###  Color Settings  #########################################################
default_shade_color grey
default_outline_color black

default_color DCDCDC #Gainsboro
color0 DAA520 #Goldenrod  FFFFF0 #Ivory
color1 778899 #LightSlateGrey
color2 FF8C00 #Darkorange
color3 7FFF00 #Chartreuse
color4 FFA07A #LightSalmon
color5 FFDEAD #NavajoWhite
color6 00BFFF #DeepSkyBlue
color7 00FFFF #Cyan 48D1CC #MediumTurquoise
color8 FFFF00 #Yellow
color9 FF0000 #Red
 #####################################################  End Color Settings  ###
 ###  Borders Section  ########################################################
draw_borders no
# Stippled borders?
stippled_borders 0
# border margins
border_inner_margin 5
# border width
border_width 0
 #####################################################  End Borders Secton  ###


 # Use the Xdbe extension? (eliminates flicker)
 # It is highly recommended to use own window with this one so
 # double buffer won't be so big
double_buffer yes

 # Adds spaces around certain objects to stop them
 # from moving other things around.
 # only works with mono fonts
use_spacer right
 # Arguments are left, right, and none (default)

 # Size of the standard text buffer (default is 256 bytes).
 # Will allow for more text display per variable.
text_buffer_size 256

 # Sets bars size even for bars that do not hace that function
 # IE: execbar and execibar
default_bar_size 335 9

 # 0 makes Conky run forever
total_run_times 0
cpu_avg_samples 1
net_avg_samples 1
no_buffers yes

 # Defaults to 4MiB
 # Set to 0 to disable the image cache
 # Increase this value if you use $image lots
imlib_cache_size 0

update_interval 3

lua_load ~/Conky/LUA/draw-bg.lua
lua_draw_hook_pre draw_bg

TEXT
${alignc}${color}Buenos Aires${color}${execi 1800 bash $HOME/accuweather_conky/accuw_script}

${font Orbitron:size=16}Today${font}
${color6}${font conkyweather:size=30}${execi 1800  sed -n '5p' ~/accuweather_conky/images}${font}${color}${goto 60}${voffset -25}${font digitalk:size=30}${execpi 1800 sed -n '1p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/temperatures}${font Orbitron:size=30}°c${font}
${execpi 1800 sed -n '1p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -s -w 22}

${font Orbitron:size=16}${execpi 1800 sed -n '1p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/days}${font}
${color6}${font conkyweather:size=30}${execi 1800  sed -n '6p' ~/accuweather_conky/images}${font}${color}${voffset -25}${goto 60}H: ${font digitalk:bold:size=15}${execpi 1800 sed -n '2p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/temperatures}${font Orbitron:size=15}°c${font}
${goto 60}L: ${font digitalk:bold:size=15}${execpi 1800 sed -n '3p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/temperatures}${font Orbitron:size=15}°c${font}
${execpi 1800 sed -n '3p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -s -w 22}

${font Orbitron:size=16}${execpi 1800 sed -n '3p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/days}${font}
${color6}${font conkyweather:size=30}${execi 1800  sed -n '7p' ~/accuweather_conky/images}${font}${color}${voffset -25}${goto 60}H: ${font digitalk:bold:size=15}${execpi 1800 sed -n '4p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/temperatures}${font Orbitron:size=15}°c${font}
${goto 60}L: ${font digitalk:bold:size=15}${execpi 1800 sed -n '5p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/temperatures}${font Orbitron:size=15}°c${font}
${execpi 1800 sed -n '5p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -s -w 22}

${font Orbitron:size=16}${execpi 1800 sed -n '5p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/days}${font}
${color6}${font conkyweather:size=30}${execi 1800  sed -n '8p' ~/accuweather_conky/images}${font}${color}${voffset -25}${goto 60}H: ${font digitalk:bold:size=15}${execpi 1800 sed -n '6p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/temperatures}${font Orbitron:size=15}°c${font}
${goto 60}L: ${font digitalk:bold:size=15}${execpi 1800 sed -n '7p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/temperatures}${font Orbitron:size=15}°c${font}
${execpi 1800 sed -n '7p' $HOME/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -s -w 22}
```

ENJOY!

Nice script Teo, very nice indeed.

Wonderful, wonderful, Wunderground is next!

### Post by Sector11 on 2011-10-22
> **BoredOutOfMyMind said:**
> Link to lua script-

> **TeoBigusGeekus said:**
> Brilliant man, good to know.
Thanks!

Actually No - that one has a memory leak that VinDSL fixed:
```
--[[	Background by londonali1010 (2009)
	VinDSL Background Hack (2010-2011)

This script draws a background to the Conky window. It covers the whole of the Conky window, but you can specify rounded corners, if you wish.

To call this script in Conky, use (assuming you have saved this script to ~/scripts/):
	lua_load ~/scripts/draw_bg.lua
	lua_draw_hook_pre draw_bg

Changelog:
	+ v3.0	VinDSL Hack (01.28.2011) Killed memory leak.
	+ v2.4	VinDSL Hack (01.25.2011) Declared all variables in local.
	+ v2.3	VinDSL Hack (12.31.2010) Added shading example(s).
	+ v2.2	VinDSL Hack (12.30.2010) Cleaned up the code a bit.
	+ v2.1	VinDSL Hack (12.24.2010) Added cairo destroy function(s).
	+ v2.0	VinDSL Hack (12.21.2010) Added height adjustment variable.
	+ v1.0	Original release (07.10.2009)

]]

-- Change these settings to affect your background:

-- "corner_r" is the radius, in pixels, of the rounded corners. If you don't want rounded corners, use 0.

	local corner_r = 25

-- Set the colour and transparency (alpha) of your background (0.00 - 0.99).

--	(Light Shading Example)
--	local bg_colour = 0x4d4d4d
--	local bg_alpha = 0.50

--	(Medium Shading Example)
--	local bg_colour = 0x222222
--	local bg_alpha = 0.50

--	(Dark Shading Example)
	local bg_colour = 0x000000
	local bg_alpha = 0.50

--	local bg_colour = 0x222222
--	local bg_alpha = 0.50

-- Tweaks the height of your background, in pixels. If you don't need to adjust the height, use 0.

--	(Default Setting)
	local vindsl_hack_height = 0
--	local vindsl_hack_height = -8

require 'cairo'
local	cs, cr = nil

local function rgb_to_r_g_b(colour,alpha)
	return ((colour / 0x10000) % 0x100) / 255., ((colour / 0x100) % 0x100) / 255., (colour % 0x100) / 255., alpha
	end

function conky_draw_bg()
	if conky_window == nil then return end
	if cs == nil then cairo_surface_destroy(cs) end
	if cr == nil then cairo_destroy(cr) end
	local w = conky_window.width
	local h = conky_window.height
	local v = vindsl_hack_height
	local cs = cairo_xlib_surface_create(conky_window.display, conky_window.drawable, conky_window.visual, conky_window.width, conky_window.height)
	local cr = cairo_create(cs)
	
	cairo_move_to(cr,corner_r,0)
	cairo_line_to(cr,w-corner_r,0)
	cairo_curve_to(cr,w,0,w,0,w,corner_r)
	cairo_line_to(cr,w,h+v-corner_r)
	cairo_curve_to(cr,w,h+v,w,h+v,w-corner_r,h+v)
	cairo_line_to(cr,corner_r,h+v)
	cairo_curve_to(cr,0,h+v,0,h+v,0,h+v-corner_r)
	cairo_line_to(cr,0,corner_r)
	cairo_curve_to(cr,0,0,0,0,corner_r,0)
	cairo_close_path(cr)

	cairo_set_source_rgba(cr,rgb_to_r_g_b(bg_colour,bg_alpha))
	cairo_fill(cr)

	cairo_surface_destroy(cs)
	cairo_destroy(cr)
	end
```

```
# killall conky && conky -c ~/TBG/WUG2/.conkyrc &


 ###  Begin Window Settings  ##################################################
 # Create own window instead of using desktop (required in nautilus)
own_window yes
own_window_type override
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_hints below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
# own_window_colour brown
own_window_class Conky
own_window_title Wunderground Bs As

 ### ARGB can be used for real transparency
 ### NOTE that a composite manager is required for real transparency.
 ### This option will not work as desired (in most cases) in conjunction with
 ### 'own_window_type override'
# own_window_argb_visual yes
 ### When ARGB visuals are enabled, this use this to modify the alpha value
 ### Valid range is 0-255, where 0 is 0% opacity, and 255 is 100% opacity. 
#own_window_argb_value 0

minimum_size 350 0 	## width, height
maximum_width 350	## width - usually a good idea to equal minimum width.

gap_x 10	## left &right
gap_y 10	## up & down

alignment top_left
 ####################################################  End Window Settings  ###
 ###  Font Settings  ##########################################################
 # Use Xft (anti-aliased font and stuff) 
use_xft yes
xftfont monospace:size=8
 # Alpha of Xft font. Must be a value at or between 1 and 0 ###
xftalpha 0
 # Force UTF8? requires XFT ###
override_utf8_locale yes

draw_shades no
draw_outline no
uppercase no

draw_outline no # amplifies text if yes
 ######################################################  End Font Settings  ###
 ###  Color Settings  #########################################################
default_shade_color grey
default_outline_color black

default_color DCDCDC #Gainsboro
color0 DAA520 #Goldenrod  FFFFF0 #Ivory
color1 778899 #LightSlateGrey
color2 FF8C00 #Darkorange
color3 7FFF00 #Chartreuse
color4 FFA07A #LightSalmon
color5 FFDEAD #NavajoWhite
color6 00BFFF #DeepSkyBlue
color7 00FFFF #Cyan 48D1CC #MediumTurquoise
color8 FFFF00 #Yellow
color9 FF0000 #Red
 #####################################################  End Color Settings  ###
 ###  Borders Section  ########################################################
draw_borders no
# Stippled borders?
stippled_borders 0
# border margins
border_inner_margin 5
# border width
border_width 0
 #####################################################  End Borders Secton  ###


 # Use the Xdbe extension? (eliminates flicker)
 # It is highly recommended to use own window with this one so
 # double buffer won't be so big
double_buffer yes

 # Adds spaces around certain objects to stop them
 # from moving other things around.
 # only works with mono fonts
use_spacer right
 # Arguments are left, right, and none (default)

 # Size of the standard text buffer (default is 256 bytes).
 # Will allow for more text display per variable.
text_buffer_size 256

 # Sets bars size even for bars that do not hace that function
 # IE: execbar and execibar
default_bar_size 335 9

 # 0 makes Conky run forever
total_run_times 0
cpu_avg_samples 1
net_avg_samples 1
no_buffers yes

 # Defaults to 4MiB
 # Set to 0 to disable the image cache
 # Increase this value if you use $image lots
imlib_cache_size 0

update_interval 3

lua_load ~/Conky/LUA/draw-bg.lua
lua_draw_hook_pre draw_bg

TEXT
${execi 600 bash $HOME/conky_wunderground2/wunderground-script}\
${color8}${execi 600  sed -n '1p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}${alignr}${color1}${execi 600 sed -n '5p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}${color}${color}

${execi 600 sed -n '3p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}${alignr -10}${execi 600 sed -n '7p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}
${execi 600 sed -n '4p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}${alignr 2}${execi 600 sed -n '8p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}
${voffset -45}${goto 110}${color8}${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '1p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
${goto 200}${color1}${execi 600  sed -n '3p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}${font}${color}
${execi 600 sed -n '2p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}${alignr}${execi 600 sed -n '6p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}
${hr}
${color8}${execi 600  sed -n '9p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}${alignr}${color1}${execi 600 sed -n '13p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}${color}${color}

${execi 600 sed -n '11p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}${alignr -10}${execi 600 sed -n '15p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}
${execi 600 sed -n '16p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}${alignr 2}${execi 600 sed -n '20p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}
${voffset -45}${goto 110}${color8}${font conkyweather:size=35}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
${goto 200}${color1}${execi 600  sed -n '7p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}${font}${color}
${execi 600 sed -n '10p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}${alignr}${execi 600 sed -n '14p' $HOME/conky_wunderground2/messages.wun}

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-10-23
> **Sector11 said:**
> Actually No - that one has a memory leak that VinDSL fixed:
```
--[[	Background by londonali1010 (2009)
	VinDSL Background Hack (2010-2011)

This script draws a background to the Conky window. It covers the whole of the Conky window, but you can specify rounded corners, if you wish.

To call this script in Conky, use (assuming you have saved this script to ~/scripts/):
	lua_load ~/scripts/draw_bg.lua
	lua_draw_hook_pre draw_bg

Changelog:
	+ v3.0	VinDSL Hack (01.28.2011) Killed memory leak.
	+ v2.4	VinDSL Hack (01.25.2011) Declared all variables in local.
	+ v2.3	VinDSL Hack (12.31.2010) Added shading example(s).
	+ v2.2	VinDSL Hack (12.30.2010) Cleaned up the code a bit.
	+ v2.1	VinDSL Hack (12.24.2010) Added cairo destroy function(s).
	+ v2.0	VinDSL Hack (12.21.2010) Added height adjustment variable.
	+ v1.0	Original release (07.10.2009)

]]

-- Change these settings to affect your background:

-- "corner_r" is the radius, in pixels, of the rounded corners. If you don't want rounded corners, use 0.

	local corner_r = 25

-- Set the colour and transparency (alpha) of your background (0.00 - 0.99).

--	(Light Shading Example)
--	local bg_colour = 0x4d4d4d
--	local bg_alpha = 0.50

--	(Medium Shading Example)
--	local bg_colour = 0x222222
--	local bg_alpha = 0.50

--	(Dark Shading Example)
	local bg_colour = 0x000000
	local bg_alpha = 0.50

--	local bg_colour = 0x222222
--	local bg_alpha = 0.50

-- Tweaks the height of your background, in pixels. If you don't need to adjust the height, use 0.

--	(Default Setting)
	local vindsl_hack_height = 0
--	local vindsl_hack_height = -8

require 'cairo'
local	cs, cr = nil

local function rgb_to_r_g_b(colour,alpha)
	return ((colour / 0x10000) % 0x100) / 255., ((colour / 0x100) % 0x100) / 255., (colour % 0x100) / 255., alpha
	end

function conky_draw_bg()
	if conky_window == nil then return end
	if cs == nil then cairo_surface_destroy(cs) end
	if cr == nil then cairo_destroy(cr) end
	local w = conky_window.width
	local h = conky_window.height
	local v = vindsl_hack_height
	local cs = cairo_xlib_surface_create(conky_window.display, conky_window.drawable, conky_window.visual, conky_window.width, conky_window.height)
	local cr = cairo_create(cs)
	
	cairo_move_to(cr,corner_r,0)
	cairo_line_to(cr,w-corner_r,0)
	cairo_curve_to(cr,w,0,w,0,w,corner_r)
	cairo_line_to(cr,w,h+v-corner_r)
	cairo_curve_to(cr,w,h+v,w,h+v,w-corner_r,h+v)
	cairo_line_to(cr,corner_r,h+v)
	cairo_curve_to(cr,0,h+v,0,h+v,0,h+v-corner_r)
	cairo_line_to(cr,0,corner_r)
	cairo_curve_to(cr,0,0,0,0,corner_r,0)
	cairo_close_path(cr)

	cairo_set_source_rgba(cr,rgb_to_r_g_b(bg_colour,bg_alpha))
	cairo_fill(cr)

	cairo_surface_destroy(cs)
	cairo_destroy(cr)
	end
```
