```

**week_2.sh**
```
#!/bin/bash

font=("\${voffset -10}\${font digitalk:size=4}" "\${voffset -0}\${font digitalk:size=8}" "\${voffset -0}\${font digitalk:size=12}" "\${voffset -3}\${font digitalk:size=30}\${color 00FFFF}" "\${voffset -15}\${font digitalk:size=12}" "\${voffset -4}\${font digitalk:size=7}" "\${voffset -2}\${font digitalk:size=4}")
color=("" "" "" "\${color}" "" "" "")

for i in $(seq -3 3); do
echo -n "${font[$[i+3]]}$(date '+%d' -d "$i days")${color[3]}\${offset 3}"
done
```

The conky on the top in the middle is a little "cheat sheet" I did to show the weather font for specific days.  It goes: day night - day night, I could have done a text file ... but I'm a conky person.
```
## killall conky && conky -c ~/TBG/conkyrc-WUG-days &

 ###  Begin Window Settings  ##################################################
 # Create own window instead of using desktop (required in nautilus)
own_window yes
own_window_type override
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_hints below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
# own_window_colour brown
own_window_class Conky
own_window_title AccuWeather Bs As

 ### ARGB can be used for real transparency
 ### NOTE that a composite manager is required for real transparency.
 ### This option will not work as desired (in most cases) in conjunction with
 ### 'own_window_type override'
# own_window_argb_visual yes
 ### When ARGB visuals are enabled, this use this to modify the alpha value
 ### Valid range is 0-255, where 0 is 0% opacity, and 255 is 100% opacity. 
#own_window_argb_value 0

# minimum_size 130 0	## width, height
# maximum_width 130	## width - usually a good idea to equal minimum width.

gap_x 10	## left &right
gap_y 10	## up & down

 ### alignment may be:
 ### top_left, top_right, top_middle, bottom_left, bottom_right, bottom_middle,
 ### middle_left, middle_middle, middle_right, or none
alignment top_middle
 ####################################################  End Window Settings  ###
 ###  Font Settings  ##########################################################
 # Use Xft (anti-aliased font and stuff) 
use_xft yes
xftfont monospace:size=8
 # Alpha of Xft font. Must be a value at or between 1 and 0 ###
xftalpha 0
 # Force UTF8? requires XFT ###
override_utf8_locale yes

draw_shades no
draw_outline no
uppercase no

draw_outline no # amplifies text if yes
 ######################################################  End Font Settings  ###
 ###  Color Settings  #########################################################
default_shade_color grey
default_outline_color black

default_color DCDCDC #Gainsboro
color0 DAA520 #Goldenrod  FFFFF0 #Ivory
color1 778899 #LightSlateGrey
color2 FF8C00 #Darkorange
color3 7FFF00 #Chartreuse
color4 FFA07A #LightSalmon
color5 FFDEAD #NavajoWhite
color6 00BFFF #DeepSkyBlue
color7 00FFFF #Cyan 48D1CC #MediumTurquoise
color8 FFFF00 #Yellow
color9 FF0000 #Red
 #####################################################  End Color Settings  ###
 ###  Borders Section  ########################################################
draw_borders no
# Stippled borders?
stippled_borders 0
# border margins
border_inner_margin 10
border_outer_margin 0
# border width
border_width 0
 #####################################################  End Borders Secton  ###


 # Use the Xdbe extension? (eliminates flicker)
 # It is highly recommended to use own window with this one so
 # double buffer won't be so big
double_buffer yes

 # Adds spaces around certain objects to stop them
 # from moving other things around.
 # only works with mono fonts
use_spacer right
 # Arguments are left, right, and none (default)

 # Size of the standard text buffer (default is 256 bytes).
 # Will allow for more text display per variable.
 # requires 512 if using week_2.sh script
text_buffer_size 512

 # Sets bars size even for bars that do not hace that function
 # IE: execbar and execibar
default_bar_size 335 9

 # 0 makes Conky run forever
total_run_times 0
cpu_avg_samples 1
net_avg_samples 1
no_buffers yes

 # Defaults to 4MiB
 # Set to 0 to disable the image cache
 # Increase this value if you use $image lots
imlib_cache_size 0

update_interval 3

lua_load ~/Conky/LUA/draw-bg.lua
lua_draw_hook_pre draw_bg

#${execi 1200  sed -n '1p' ~/conky_wunderground2/moon | fold -s -w 21}

TEXT
Day 1    Day 2
 1  3    5  7
${font conkyweather:size=15}${execi 600  sed -n '1p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
 ${execi 600  sed -n '3p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
   ${execi 600  sed -n '5p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
 ${execi 600  sed -n '7p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}${font}
Day 3    Day 4
 9  1    13  15
${font conkyweather:size=15}${execi 600  sed -n '9p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
 ${execi 600  sed -n '11p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
   ${execi 600  sed -n '13p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
 ${execi 600  sed -n '15p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}${font}
Day 5    Day 6
17  19   21  23
${font conkyweather:size=15}${execi 600  sed -n '17p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
 ${execi 600  sed -n '19p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
   ${execi 600  sed -n '21p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
 ${execi 600  sed -n '23p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}${font}
Day 7    Day Ex
25  27   29  31
${font conkyweather:size=15}${execi 600  sed -n '25p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
 ${execi 600  sed -n '27p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
   ${execi 600  sed -n '29p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}\
 ${execi 600  sed -n '31p' ~/conky_wunderground2/weather_icons}
${font}
```

### Post by mdbelt on 2011-10-30
Hey great stuff.  Kudos to this great effort.  I wanted to chip in my 2 cents.

First I started with your great examples.
Then I wanted to use wireless network & the metar weather stations for super current information from my airport (in addition to Accuweather forecast), so I needed to compile latest conky.

To compile:

1) Install Dependencies
```
sudo apt-get build-dep conky

```
2) Download Latest Conky Source to /usr/src/
[http://conky.sourceforge.net/](http://conky.sourceforge.net/)

3) Compile
```
sudo ./configure --prefix=/usr --enable-wlan --enable-iostats --enable-weather-metar
```

4) Make
```
sudo make

```
5) If you don't get weird error message then proceed.  Otherwise stop & do some research.  Probably a dependency problem or something.

6) Remove any previous versions you got from apt-get
```
sudo apt-get remove conky

```
7) Install
```
sudo make install

```

Bingo bango :D, you can now use wireless lan & metar

I used the following lines for my wireless data:

```
D: $color${downspeed wlan0}/s ${alignr}U: ${upspeed wlan0}/s
DT: ${totaldown wlan0} ${alignr}UT: ${totalup wlan0}
BR: ${wireless_bitrate wlan0} ${alignr}LQ: ${wireless_link_qual wlan0}%

```
Edit:  I forgot about the whole fahrenheit thing.  Add this towards the top unless you want the C which is default:
```
temperature_unit fahrenheit
```

I used the following for current metar weather info:

```
${goto 75}Current:
${goto 75}Temp: ${weather http://weather.noaa.gov/pub/data/observations/metar/stations/ KSTL temperature temperature 30}F
${goto 75}Pressure:${weather http://weather.noaa.gov/pub/data/observations/metar/stations/ KSTL pressure pressure 30}mb
${goto 75}Wind Speed:${weather http://weather.noaa.gov/pub/data/observations/metar/stations/ KSTL wind_speed wind_speed 30}
${goto 75}Wind Direction:${weather http://weather.noaa.gov/pub/data/observations/metar/stations/ KSTL wind_dir wind_dir 30}
${goto 75}Humidity:${weather http://weather.noaa.gov/pub/data/observations/metar/stations/ KSTL humidity humidity 30}
${goto 75}Misc:${weather http://weather.noaa.gov/pub/data/observations/metar/stations/ KSTL weather weather 30}

```
To get the correct ICAO code for your airport you can search here:
[http://www.airlinecodes.co.uk/aptcodesearch.asp](http://www.airlinecodes.co.uk/aptcodesearch.asp)  (for Location you can just type the city name eg: Saint Louis & look for the ICAO-Code in the results)
Replace "KSTL" above with your 4 digit airport code

I'm new to this stuff.  Stiched together from a bunch of forums (this forum was the jumping off point).  Hope this is helpful.

MB

You don't have to recode conky from source to use metar. Maybe I posted on The Dark Side what codes worked. 

sudo apt-get install weather-utils

Add these lines above TEXT. 
> # and for US users who are Metrically challenged
# blame BoredOOMM
temperature_unit fahrenheit

lua_load /home/bkberger/Conky/LUA/draw-bg.lua
lua_draw_hook_pre draw_bg

# stuff after 'TEXT' will be formatted on screen

TEXT

The variables are here- 

[http://conky.sourceforge.net/variables.html](http://conky.sourceforge.net/variables.html)

### Post by Presence on 2011-10-31
Having some issues with my .conkyrc. The line ${execi 600 bash /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/accuw_USA_script} doesn't seem to run the whole script. It runs the wget command, but the grep and sed commands are not run, so the forecast is not updating automatically. The script runs fine on its own.

Also getting a segmentation fault when starting up conky right after booting up, though the problem disappears with no noticeable changes a few minutes later. 

Maybe I'm just overlooking something with both of these issues. I'll try working on it later.


If you want to use the script, make sure you change home folder and the location url to match yours. Note that the page it pulls is now "forecast-current-conditions.asp" instead of "forecast-details.asp". This shouldn't affect the 5 day forecast.

Also note that I use the folder ".accuweather_conky_USA" so the folder is hidden. You'll need to change that if you are using something different.



EDIT: Updated script with sunrise/sunset and moonrise/moonset.

Here's the modified script:

```
#!/bin/bash

#function: test_image
test_image () {
	case $1 in
	 c|d|f)
	   echo e
	 ;;
	 fl|sn)
	   echo q
	 ;;
	 cl)
	   echo A
	 ;;
	 pc)
	   echo C
	 ;;
	 co)
	   echo E
	 ;;
	 fr|r)
	   echo i
	 ;;
	 h|ic)
	   echo b
	 ;;
	 ho)
	   echo 5
	 ;;
	 i)
	   echo r
	 ;;
	 mcl)
	   echo B
	 ;;
	 mc)
	   echo c
	 ;;
	 mcfl|mcsn|psfl)
	   echo o
	 ;;
	 mcs)
	   echo g
	 ;;
	 pcs)
	   echo G
	 ;;
	 mct|psut)
	   echo k
	 ;;
	 pct)
	   echo K
	 ;;
	 msu|psu)
	   echo b
	 ;;
	 psus)
	   echo g
	 ;;
	 s)
	   echo h
	 ;;
	 rsn|sl)
	   echo x
	 ;;
	 su)
	   echo a
	 ;;
	 t)
	   echo m
	 ;;
	 w)
	   echo 6
	 ;;
	esac
} 

wget -O ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/raw http://www.accuweather.com/us/YOUR/LOCATION/HERE/forecast-current-conditions.asp
cp ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/raw ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw
sed -i '/feed-tabs/,/\/.feed-tabs/!d' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/raw

#file: days
grep '#' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/raw > ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/days
sed -i 's/<\|>/\n/g' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/days
sed -i '/Today\|Tonight\|Overnight\|Tomorrow\|Monday\|Tuesday\|Wednesday\|Thursday\|Friday\|Saturday\|Sunday/!d' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/days

#file: messages
grep 'cond' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/raw > ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/messages
sed -i 's/<\|>/\n/g' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/messages
sed -i '/span class="cond"\|\/span/d' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/messages

#file: temperatures
grep 'temp' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/raw > ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures
sed -i 's/"temp">\|<span>&\|&\|<\/span>\ /\n/g' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures
sed -i '/<strong class=\|deg;<\/span><\/strong><span class="low"><span>Lo\|deg;<\/span>/d' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures
day1=$(sed -n '1p' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/days)
if [[ $day1 == Overnight || $day1 == Tonight ]]; then
   sed -i 1i"-" ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures
fi

#file: images
grep 'icon' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/raw > ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images
sed -i 's/\ i-alarm//g' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images
sed -i 's/\ i-\|"></\n/g' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images
sed -i '/<div class="icon\|\/div>/d' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images
for (( i=1; i<=5; i++ ))
  do
   test_number=$(sed -n "${i}p" ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images)
   test_image $test_number >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images

  done
messag1=$(sed -n '1p' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/days)
if [[ $messag1 == Tonight || $messag1 == Overnight ]]; then
   imag1=$(sed -n '1p' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images)
   case $imag1 in
    h|ic)
      sed -i '6s/b/B/' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    mc)
      sed -i '6s/c/C/' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    mcfl|mcsn)
      sed -i '6s/o/O/' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    mcs)
      sed -i '6s/g/G/' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    mct)
      sed -i '6s/k/K/' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    esac
fi


#current conditions
sed -i '/#detail-tab-buttons/,/\/#panel-main/!d' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw

#file: current
grep 'realfeel' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw > ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Humidity:' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Pressure:' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Cloud Cover:' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'UV Index:' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Dew Point:' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Precipitation' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Visibility:' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep '"more-info"' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'position:absolute;' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'start' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'finish' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep '="icon' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current

sed -i 's/[ ]*<div class="icon i-//' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current

sed -i -e 's/[ ]*<div[a-zA-Z0-9 =":;-]*>//g' -e 's/[ ]*<span[a-zA-Z0-9 =":;-]*>//g' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/<\/span><\/span>/<\/span>/g' -e 's/<\/span>/\n/g' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/[ ]*<li>[a-zA-Z0-9 ()]*: <strong>//g' -e 's/<\/strong><\/li>//g' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/&nbsp;/ /g' -e 's/&deg;//g' -e 's/<\/div>//g' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current

sed -i -e 's/RealFeel&reg; //' -e 's/ <img src="\/adc2010\/images\/icons-wind\///' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/.gif" width="260" height="138" alt="" \/>//' -e 's/">//' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current

sed -i -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) in/\1/g' -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) Miles/\1/g' -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) mph/\1/g' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) F/\1/g' -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) %/\1/g' -e 's/\([0-9.]*\)%/\1/g' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current


#If you want AM and PM in uppercase, comment this line out
sed -i -e 's/ AM/am/g' -e 's/ PM/pm/g' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current

test_number=$(sed -n "22p" ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current)
test_image $test_number >> ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current

```




And my .conkyrc:

```
alignment top_left
background no
border_width 1
cpu_avg_samples 2

#colors
default_color ffffff
color0 40abc5        #titles
color1 cccccc        #sub-titles
color2 cccccc        #processes
color3 556776        #graphs


default_outline_color white
default_shade_color white
draw_borders no
draw_graph_borders yes
draw_outline no
draw_shades no
use_xft yes
xftfont Liberation Mono:size=7.5
gap_x 1330
gap_y 30
minimum_size 315 680
net_avg_samples 2
no_buffers yes
out_to_console no
out_to_stderr no
extra_newline no
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_argb_visual yes
own_window_type normal
own_window_class conky-semi
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
stippled_borders 0
update_interval 1.0
uppercase no
use_spacer none
show_graph_scale no
show_graph_range no
double_buffer yes





TEXT
${font Liberation Sans:size=60}${time %l:%M%P}${font}


    ${font Liberation Sans:size=14}${time %A}         ${time %B %e, %Y}
${font}
${goto 40}Sunrise: ${execpi 600 sed -n '14p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}   ${goto 170}Moonrise: ${execpi 600 sed -n '16p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}
${goto 40}Sunset:  ${execpi 600 sed -n '18p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}   ${goto 170}Moonset:  ${execpi 600 sed -n '20p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}

${goto 20}${color0}${font Nimbus Sans L:bold:size=10}CURRENT $font$color${hr}${texeci 600 bash /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/accuw_USA_script}

${goto 40}${font conkyweather:size=40}${color3}${execi 600  sed -n '23p' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}${color}
${font Nimbus Sans L:size=30}${goto 120}${voffset -75}${execpi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}°
${font}${goto 120}${voffset -33}${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current|fold -w30}

${goto 120}Wind:           ${execpi 600 sed -n '12p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current} ${execpi 600 sed -n '13p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}mph
${goto 120}Feels Like:     ${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}°
${goto 120}Humidity:       ${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}%
${goto 120}Pressure:       ${execpi 600 sed -n '6p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}in
${goto 120}Cloud Cover:    ${execpi 600 sed -n '7p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}%
${goto 120}UV Index:       ${execpi 600 sed -n '8p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}
${goto 120}Dew Point:      ${execpi 600 sed -n '9p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}°
${goto 120}Precipitation:  ${execpi 600 sed -n '10p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}in
${goto 120}Visibility:     ${execpi 600 sed -n '11p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current} miles


${goto 20}${color0}${font Nimbus Sans L:bold:size=10}FORECAST $font$color${hr}

${goto 40}${font conkyweather:size=40}${color3}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images}${color}${font Nimbus Sans L:bold:size=8}${goto 120}${voffset -40}${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/days}
${font}${goto 120}${execpi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/messages|fold -w30}
${goto 120}High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures}°  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures}°

${goto 40}${font conkyweather:size=40}${color3}${execi 600  sed -n '7p' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images}${color}${font Nimbus Sans L:bold:size=8}${goto 120}${voffset -35}${execpi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/days}
${font}${goto 120}${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/messages|fold -w30}

${goto 40}${font conkyweather:size=40}${color3}${execi 600  sed -n '8p' ~/.accuweather_conky_USA/images}${color}${font Nimbus Sans L:bold:size=8}${goto 120}${voffset -35}${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/days}

I have a question for anyone that might know (this might not even be possible with conky)... 

Currently when conky updates the script, both the script and sed commands are run at the same time, but since the script takes a second or so to download the html file and update the information, the values on conky are not updated until the next time it's run. 

Is there a way to offset when the sed commands are run, say 5 seconds, compared to the script? This way the values would be updated as soon as the script was finished running.

### Post by Presence on 2011-11-01
No, talking about in the conkyrc itself.

I tried adding this:

${execpi 300 **sleep 5 &&** sed -n '12p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}

### Post by 42dorian on 2011-11-01
> **Presence said:**
> No, talking about in the conkyrc itself.

I tried adding this:

${execpi 300 **sleep 5 &&** sed -n '12p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}

That's not very efficient though and doesn't work well. 

This isn't a huge deal, it's probably not possible the way conky is set up.

Easier than that.  Just call the execpi less often.  Like... 600 instead of 300.  That command actually tells Linux to run the script separately in it's own background process and report the results to Conky.  So, to delay it, increase the interval between calls.

---

### Post by BoredOutOfMyMind on 2011-11-01
> **42dorian said:**
> Easier than that.  Just call the execpi less often.  Like... 600 instead of 300.  That command actually tells Linux to run the script separately in it's own background process and report the results to Conky.  So, to delay it, increase the interval between calls.

Actually he is saying the conky loads the top half and all the images at the bottom then must wait on the script to be defined.

### Post by Presence on 2011-11-01
I don't think you guys are understanding what I'm trying to do.

To simplify it, let's say I have just these two commands:

```
${texeci 600 bash /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/accuw_USA_script}
${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}
```

Lets say its time point 0 so both commands are executed. The problem is the second command grabs data from a file that's updated by the first, and the first command takes a couple seconds to execute fully (let's say 2 seconds for this example). The file isn't ready until time point 2, but the second command tried to update it at time point 0, so it wasn't updated. This means the data won't be updated until the command is run again at time point 600. 

I still want both commands to run at the same interval, but for one command to be delayed in relation to the other one.


What I've done right now is set the script to an interval of 900 and the sed commands to an interval of 300. This means it only takes 5 minutes to update the conky information after the script has been run. However, the sed commands are being run more often than necessary.

I changed the FIRST {exec 300.... line and changed all other {execi 300.... to {texeci 300.... 

The Sunrise and Sunset have {execpi 600......
I had to hit save again in gedit to get the Sunrise info to fill in. conkyLatLong has a HUGE timeout as it will not be updated until I move next February. 86400 seconds is a whole day. 

> {goto 10}Lat/Lon: ${execi 86400 conkyLatLong}
${execi 300 bash /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/accuw_USA_script}
 
From [The Conky MAN]("http://conky.sourceforge.net/docs.html")

> **exec** command
Executes a shell command and displays the output in conky. warning: this takes a lot more resources than other variables. I'd recommend coding wanted behaviour in C and posting a patch.

**execi **interval command
Same as exec but with specific interval. Interval can't be less than update_interval in configuration. See also $texeci

**execpi** interval command
Same as execp but with specific interval. Interval can't be less than update_interval in configuration. Note that the output from the $execpi command is still parsed and evaluated at every interval.

**texeci** interval command
Runs a command at an interval inside a thread and displays the output. Same as $execi, except the command is run inside a thread. Use this if you have a slow script to keep Conky updating. You should make the interval slightly longer then the time it takes your script to execute. For example, if you have a script that take 5 seconds to execute, you should make the interval at least 6 seconds. See also $execi. This object will clean up the thread when it is destroyed, so it can safely be used in a nested fashion, though it may not produce the desired behaviour if used this way.

### Post by 42dorian on 2011-11-02
> **Presence said:**
> I don't think you guys are understanding what I'm trying to do.

To simplify it, let's say I have just these two commands:

```
${texeci 600 bash /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/accuw_USA_script}
${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/dan/.accuweather_conky_USA/current}
```

Lets say its time point 0 so both commands are executed. The problem is the second command grabs data from a file that's updated by the first, and the first command takes a couple seconds to execute fully (let's say 2 seconds for this example). The file isn't ready until time point 2, but the second command tried to update it at time point 0, so it wasn't updated. This means the data won't be updated until the command is run again at time point 600. 

I still want both commands to run at the same interval, but for one command to be delayed in relation to the other one.


What I've done right now is set the script to an interval of 900 and the sed commands to an interval of 300. This means it only takes 5 minutes to update the conky information after the script has been run. However, the sed commands are being run more often than necessary.

Well, no.  You don't want the scripts to be run at the same time.  As you said, you want a delay.  You put that delay in by changing the interval they're executed.  Changing it to a texeci type command will run the script itself in it's own background thread, regardless of how many updates Conky does.  The regular intervals of all the sed calls are what update Conky as to what the texeci call of the script has come up with since last update.

Regardless of how you do it, the script is a script, not a live feed.  You won't get real time data from the site anyways, and there will always be a few delays due to the runtime.

It does sound like you guys both need to double your user text space and number of specials in your .conkyrc environment variables.  It may well be that empty space you're getting is due to the scripts you're using are, perhaps, a few characters too long for the environment variables to like.  Ironically, once the script has run, and generated some output for you boys, it doesn't add to the total script size allowed by the environment variables.  Since it's not always running, you're getting data for it once in a while, and the total script size and user text space is shorter on those intervals where the script has already run and the sed commands are being redundantly run on their own, reading it's output.

The fact remains, the means to change the interval at which the sed commands and the main script run are at your fingertips.  Since it is a script and not a live feed, there will always be bugs in the live feed abilities.  But, considering how well it already works, I think you might just be asking too much of your intervals.  Increasing them all as a whole may produce better results.  Not to make any direct comparisons, but the old conkyForecast python script, the average run interval was 1800 or so.  That's in seconds.  Perhaps 300, 600, or 900 seconds is too fast for the script to run efficiently and you just need to run everything slower?  Usual weather data is only updated around 30 minutes or so.  That's 1800 seconds.  Maybe you should try that interval out and see how often you get the blank sections?

---

### Post by Presence on 2011-11-03
There was no issue with blank spaces appearing, so no need to change the buffer size (I'm assuming that's what you're referring to).

The main issue here was that the values that were saved before shutting down would appear after booting and would remain there until the next time conky updated. Changing the intervals would have no effect on that since all commands would still be run at time point 0. 

What I did to work around this was just create a script to launch conky and have the accuweather script run once before conky was loaded. A delay would be more ideal, but I'm done messing with it. It works fine now as it is.

---

### Post by 42dorian on 2011-11-03
> **Presence said:**
> There was no issue with blank spaces appearing, so no need to change the buffer size (I'm assuming that's what you're referring to).

The main issue here was that the values that were saved before shutting down would appear after booting and would remain there until the next time conky updated. Changing the intervals would have no effect on that since all commands would still be run at time point 0. 

What I did to work around this was just create a script to launch conky and have the accuweather script run once before conky was loaded. A delay would be more ideal, but I'm done messing with it. It works fine now as it is.

Yeah, sorry, between you and BOOMM, I got the impression that there were gaps showing when you started.  As I said, it's what it sounded like.

You could also edit your script/scripts to detect the icon and results files if they exist, and delete them.  Then, increase the interval of the script, but not the sed calls.  Then, the script would run, removing old results, and the sed calls wouldn't get the old results, they'd get the new script run's results.

No matter how you slice it, the issue you're running into is a limitation of how the script works, not how conky works.  The script is a fantastic thing, but you have to accept that it's a script and not a separate application.  If you run Conky long enough, the sed calls and the script itself will run into a simultaneous runtime, resulting in bad data until the next run of one or the other.  That's just a limitation of the script.  It's a very well made script, and even then you have to accept that it is a script with an interval.  Considering weather.com is essentially shutting us all down, I would say accept the bad data as a "Better Than Having To Pay $20 a Month to Weather.com" cost.

accuw_USA_script
```
#!/bin/bash

#function: test_image
test_image () {
	case $1 in
	 c|d|f)
	   echo e
	 ;;
	 fl|sn)
	   echo q
	 ;;
	 cl)
	   echo A
	 ;;
	 pc)
	   echo C
	 ;;
	 co)
	   echo E
	 ;;
	 fr|r)
	   echo i
	 ;;
	 h|ic)
	   echo b
	 ;;
	 ho)
	   echo 5
	 ;;
	 i)
	   echo r
	 ;;
	 mcl)
	   echo B
	 ;;
	 mc)
	   echo c
	 ;;
	 mcfl|mcsn|psfl)
	   echo o
	 ;;
	 mcs)
	   echo g
	 ;;
	 pcs)
	   echo G
	 ;;
	 mct|psut)
	   echo k
	 ;;
	 pct)
	   echo K
	 ;;
	 msu|psu)
	   echo b
	 ;;
	 psus)
	   echo g
	 ;;
	 s)
	   echo h
	 ;;
	 rsn|sl)
	   echo x
	 ;;
	 su)
	   echo a
	 ;;
	 t)
	   echo m
	 ;;
	 w)
	   echo 6
	 ;;
	esac
} 

wget -O /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/raw http://www.accuweather.com/us/co/cheyenne-wells/80810/forecast-current-conditions.asp
cp /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/raw /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw
sed -i '/feed-tabs/,/\/.feed-tabs/!d' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/raw

#file: days
grep '#' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/raw > /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/days
sed -i 's/<\|>/\n/g' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/days
sed -i '/Today\|Tonight\|Overnight\|Tomorrow\|Monday\|Tuesday\|Wednesday\|Thursday\|Friday\|Saturday\|Sunday/!d' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/days

#file: messages
grep 'cond' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/raw > /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/messages
sed -i 's/<\|>/\n/g' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/messages
sed -i '/span class="cond"\|\/span/d' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/messages

#file: temperatures
grep 'temp' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/raw > /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures
sed -i 's/"temp">\|<span>&\|&\|<\/span>\ /\n/g' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures
sed -i '/<strong class=\|deg;<\/span><\/strong><span class="low"><span>Lo\|deg;<\/span>/d' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures
day1=$(sed -n '1p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/days)
if [[ $day1 == Overnight || $day1 == Tonight ]]; then
   sed -i 1i"-" /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures
fi

#file: images
grep 'icon' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/raw > /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/images
sed -i 's/\ i-alarm//g' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/images
sed -i 's/\ i-\|"></\n/g' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/images
sed -i '/<div class="icon\|\/div>/d' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/images
for (( i=1; i<=5; i++ ))
  do
   test_number=$(sed -n "${i}p" /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/images)
   test_image $test_number >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/images

  done
messag1=$(sed -n '1p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/days)
if [[ $messag1 == Tonight || $messag1 == Overnight ]]; then
   imag1=$(sed -n '1p' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/images)
   case $imag1 in
    h|ic)
      sed -i '6s/b/B/' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    mc)
      sed -i '6s/c/C/' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    mcfl|mcsn)
      sed -i '6s/o/O/' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    mcs)
      sed -i '6s/g/G/' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    mct)
      sed -i '6s/k/K/' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    esac
fi


#current conditions
sed -i '/#detail-tab-buttons/,/\/#panel-main/!d' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw

#file: current
grep 'realfeel' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw > /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Humidity:' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Pressure:' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Cloud Cover:' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'UV Index:' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Dew Point:' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Precipitation' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Visibility:' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep '"more-info"' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'position:absolute;' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'start' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'finish' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep '="icon' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current

sed -i 's/[ ]*<div class="icon i-//' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current

sed -i -e 's/[ ]*<div[a-zA-Z0-9 =":;-]*>//g' -e 's/[ ]*<span[a-zA-Z0-9 =":;-]*>//g' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/<\/span><\/span>/<\/span>/g' -e 's/<\/span>/\n/g' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/[ ]*<li>[a-zA-Z0-9 ()]*: <strong>//g' -e 's/<\/strong><\/li>//g' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/&nbsp;/ /g' -e 's/&deg;//g' -e 's/<\/div>//g' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current

sed -i -e 's/RealFeel&reg; //' -e 's/ <img src="\/adc2010\/images\/icons-wind\///' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/.gif" width="260" height="138" alt="" \/>//' -e 's/">//' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current

sed -i -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) in/\1/g' -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) Miles/\1/g' -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) mph/\1/g' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) F/\1/g' -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) %/\1/g' -e 's/\([0-9.]*\)%/\1/g' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current


#If you want AM and PM in uppercase, comment this line out
sed -i -e 's/ AM/am/g' -e 's/ PM/pm/g' /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current

test_number=$(sed -n "22p" /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current)
test_image $test_number >> /home/bkberger/accuweather_conky_USA/current
```

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-11-04
> **abumaia said:**
> Did WUnderground change something again? I just tried to use your script, and half of the output is N/A. I looked in the ICS file, and here's what I get for the first two entries:

It's spelling everything out, not giving discrete data. I hope to get this working, as WUnderground is where I'm sending data to from my backyard weather station. This is the only way I've found to get that data displayed, so far.

Sorry mate, it's my mistake: wunderground doesn't work for usa locations
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=8258002#post8258002]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=8258002#post8258002")
I'll correct the script's title at the first page.

### Post by roc4fun on 2011-11-04
> **abumaia said:**
> YepYep, got that added correctly.
I'm using the .conkyrc from the download archive, just to test before attempting further customization.

Screenshot is here: [http://dl.dropbox.com/u/8725574/Screenshot%20at%202011-11-04%2000%3A20%3A58.png](http://dl.dropbox.com/u/8725574/Screenshot%20at%202011-11-04%2000%3A20%3A58.png)

abumaia, wunderground works just fine, you'll have to put together your own script...

I'm getting my current temp and sunrise/sunset like this...

```
#!/bin/bash


wget -O ~/accuweather_conky_USA/wun http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:64110.1.99999
sed -i '/id="layerCurrent"/,/\/nowMoon/!d' ~/accuweather_conky_USA/wun

#file: currenttemp
grep -i 'tempActual' ~/accuweather_conky_USA/wun | cut -c140-141 > ~/accuweather_conky_USA/currenttemp 
#cut -c139-143 ~/accuweather_conky_USA/currenttemp

#file: nowSuns
egrep -i '"sRise"|"sSet"' ~/accuweather_conky_USA/wun > ~/accuweather_conky_USA/nowSuns

```

If you look at Teo's accuweather script and conky as an example you can see how it works... the wget statement gets the data and and puts it in a file.  In my case wun.

The next statements get data from the wun file and places it in another file which I then parse from my conky.

```
${execi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/dave/accuweather_conky_USA/currenttemp}
```

and 

```
Sunrise: ${execi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/dave/accuweather_conky_USA/nowSuns | cut -c35-38} AM
```

See if this makes sense to you.

Dave

Go to [www.accuweather.com]("www.accuweather.com"), type in your location and press enter. If your location is found, you should be directed to a page that looks like this:

Copy and keep the web address somewhere.


Go also to the toppish left corner of the page, on the forecast menu, and make sure that the page "This Week" exists.
The script makes use of it for the seven days forecast (you don't have to copy its address, the script will find it automatically).

```
#!/bin/bash

#function: test_image
test_image () {
	case $1 in
	 1)
	   echo 1su
	 ;;
	 2)
	   echo 2msu
	 ;;
	 3)
	   echo 3psu
	 ;;
     4)
       echo 4ic
     ;;
     5)
       echo 5h
     ;;
	 6)
	   echo 6mc
	 ;;
	 7)
	   echo 7c
	 ;;
     8)
       echo 8d
     ;;
	 11)
	   echo 9f
	 ;;
	 12)
	   echo 10s
	 ;;
	 13)
	   echo 11mcs
	 ;;
     14)
       echo 12psus
     ;;
	 15)
	   echo 13t
	 ;;
	 16)
	   echo 14mct
	 ;;
     17)
       echo 15psut
     ;;
	 18)
	   echo 16r
	 ;;
	 19)
	   echo 17fl
	 ;;
	 20)
	   echo 18mcfl
	 ;;
     21)
       echo 19psfl
     ;;
	 22)
	   echo 20sn
	 ;;
     23)
       echo 21mcsn
     ;;
	 24)
	   echo 22i
	 ;;
	 25)
	   echo 23sl
	 ;;
     26)
       echo 24fr
     ;;
	 29)
	   echo 25rsn
	 ;;
     30)
	   echo 27ho
	 ;;
     31)
       echo 28co
     ;;
	 32)
	   echo 26w
	 ;;
	 33)
	   echo 29cl
	 ;;
	 34)
	   echo 31mcl
	 ;;
	 35)
	   echo 32pc
	 ;;
     36)
       echo 33ic
     ;;
     37)
       echo 34h
     ;;
     38)
       echo 35mc
     ;;
	 39)
	   echo 36pcs
	 ;;
     40)
       echo 37mcs
     ;;
	 41)
	   echo 38pct
	 ;;
     42)
       echo 39mct
     ;;
	 43)
	   echo 40mcfl
	 ;;
     44)
       echo 41mcsn
     ;;
	esac
} 

#put your Accuweather address here
address="http://www.accuweather.com/en-us/ar/buenos+aires+-+capital+federal/buenos+aires/quick-look.aspx?cityid=7894"

kill -STOP $(pidof conky)
killall wget
rm /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/*.png
rm /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages_raw
wget -O /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond_raw $address
addr_week=$(echo $address|sed 's/quick.*$/forecast.aspx/')
wget -O /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week_raw $addr_week

#Current Conditions - curr_cond file
egrep -i 'CurrentTemp|CurrentText|RealFeelValue|WindsValue|HumidityValue|DewPointValue|PressureValue|PressureTenValue|VisibilityValue|SunriseValue|SunsetValue|imgCurConCondition' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond_raw > /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond
sed -i '1s/^.*blue\///' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond
sed -i '1s/_int.*$//' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond
sed -i 's/^.*">//g' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond
sed -i 's/<\/span>.*$//g' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond
sed -i 's/&deg;C//g' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond
curr_cond_raw_image=$(sed -n 1p /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond)
sed -i 1s/$curr_cond_raw_image/$(test_image $curr_cond_raw_image)/ /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond
cp /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/Forecast_Images/$(sed -n 1p /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond).png /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/cc.png
sed -i 's/Unavailable/N\/A/g' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond

#Forecast of the week - week file
egrep -i 'monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday|sunday|&deg|lblDesc|imgIcon' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week_raw > /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week
sed -i '1d' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week
sed -i -e 's/^.*lblDate">//g' -e 's/^.*lblDesc">//g' -e 's/^.*Label1">//g' -e 's/^.*lblRealFeel">//g' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week
sed -i -e 's/^.*lblHigh">//g' -e 's/^.*lblRealFeelValue">//g' -e 's/^.*blue\///g' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week
sed -i 's/_int.jpg.*$//g' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week
sed -i 's/<\/span>.*$//g' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week
sed -i 's/&deg;C//g' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week
sed -i -e 's/Monday/MONDAY/' -e 's/Tuesday/TUESDAY/' -e 's/Wednesday/WEDNESDAY/' -e 's/Thursday/THURSDAY/' -e 's/Friday/FRIDAY/' -e 's/Saturday/SATURDAY/' -e 's/Sunday/SUNDAY/' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week
for (( i=2; i<=67; i+=5 ))
  do
      sed -i "${i}s/ .*$//" /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week
  done
for (( i=1; i<=66; i+=5 ))
  do
      image_raw=$(sed -n "${i}"p /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week)
      sed -i ${i}s/$image_raw/$(test_image $image_raw)/ /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week
      cp /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/Forecast_Images/$(sed -n ${i}p /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week).png /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/$i.png

  done
  
#messages and messages_curr files
for (( i=3; i<=68; i+=5 ))
  do
      sed -n ${i}p /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week >> /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages_raw
  done
sed -n 1p /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages_raw | cut -c -60 > /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages_curr
sed -n 8p /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages_raw | cut -c -60 >> /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages_curr
for (( i=1; i<=4; i++))
	do
		no=$(sed -n ${i}p /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages_curr|wc -c)
		if (( no<31 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/$/\n/" /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages_curr
			i=$((i+1))
		elif (( no>=31 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/^\(.\{31\}\)/\1\n/" /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages_curr
			i=$((i+1))
		fi
	done
cat /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages_raw| cut -c -40 > /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages
for (( i=1; i<=28; i++))
	do
		no=$(sed -n ${i}p /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages|wc -c)
		if (( no<21 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/$/\n/" /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages
			i=$((i+1))
		elif (( no>=21 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/^\(.\{21\}\)/\1\n/" /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages
			i=$((i+1))
		fi
	done

kill -CONT $(pidof conky)
```

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-11-04
You're very unlucky: accuweather doesn't have a week forecast for your place.
If you press the "This week" (or "Next week") button, you get redirected to accuweather's main page.
I find it quite strange though; Kastoria (tiny, puny, little nothing of a town) gets a long forecast and Buenos Aires (humongous) doesn't.

### Post by Sector11 on 2011-11-04
> **TeoBigusGeekus said:**
> I've found what the mistake is (I think). If you block cookies, accuweather redirects you to its home page.
I have to make wget accept cookies.

> If you cannot use --load-cookies, there might still be an
alternative. If your browser supports a "cookie manager", you can
use it to view the cookies used when accessing the site you&#8217;re
mirroring. Write down the name and value of the cookie, and
manually instruct Wget to send those cookies, bypassing the
"official" cookie support:

wget --no-cookies --header "Cookie: <name>=<value>"
That part looks interesting

The only thing I know about cookies is IceWeasel kills them, except for a select few, and I like chocolate chips in the oatmeal ones.

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-11-04
Aha!!!! Found it!!!!
Replace these lines:
```
wget -O /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images/curr_cond_raw $address
addr_week=$(echo $address|sed 's/quick.*$/forecast.aspx/')
wget -O /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images/week_raw $addr_week
```
with these
```
wget --save-cookies /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images/cookie -O /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images/curr_cond_raw $address
addr_week=$(echo $address|sed 's/quick.*$/forecast.aspx/')
wget --load-cookies /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images/cookie -O /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images/week_raw $addr_week
```
This way, wget will save a cookie while loading the first url and use it while loading the second.

Here's Buenos Aires weather:

---

### Post by prupert on 2011-11-04
Hi

I don't mean to sound ungrateful, but please don't put files in the root of a tar'ed file that could overwrite important user files....like .conkyrc.

As I'm using a server I ssh into as it has no keyboard/mouse, I just downloaded your wunderground attachment via wget and untarred it and it has now overwritten my own .conkyrc, a file that I have been working on for months. Yes, I should have had a backup, but I never expected a .conkyrc to be contained in the root of the tar file.

Needless to say, I am pretty peeved. Yes, it's my fault for wgetting and untarring in my home folder and not having a back up, but still, dude.....

I don't mean to detract from the no doubt awesome work you have done here, but still...grrr!

---

### Post by abumaia on 2011-11-04
Ok, I've decided to use the NWS script for forecast data, and data from my station's currdat.lst for current conditions. Only problem is I do not know how to set up the sed to pull a specific piece of data. The current outdoor temp reported by my station is listed as deg_F = "52.0". How do I set up the sed to grab only the data between the quotes? I've already figured out how to get it to read the right line.

Edit: Nevermind, I figured it out after several hours of trial and error: sed -n '43 s/.*"\(.*\)"/\1/p' currdat.lst

Now I just have to be careful which font I use. For some reason Conky is putting that rectangle box character just after the sed data. It does not appear when I send the sed output to a text file, so it has to be Conky's fault.

---

                <strong class="temp">57<span>&deg;</span></strong><span class="low"><span>Lo</span> 40&deg;</span> 
              </div> 
              <!-- /.info --> 
                 
               
              <a href="http://www.accuweather.com/us/ny/whitney-point/13862/forecast-details.asp?fday=4" class="bt-more">more</a> 
               
            </div> 
            <!-- /.bg --> 
            </li> 
   
            <li class="day hv last cl { href: 'http://www.accuweather.com/us/ny/whitney-point/13862/forecast-details.asp?fday=5' }"> 
            <div class="frame"></div> 
             
            <div class="bg bg-c"> 
              <h3><a href="#">Tuesday</a></h3> 
              <h4>Nov 8</h4> 
 
              <div class="icon i-psu"></div> 
               
              <div class="info"> 
                <span class="cond">Partial sunshine</span> 
                <strong class="temp">60<span>&deg;</span></strong><span class="low"><span>Lo</span> 43&deg;</span> 
              </div> 
              <!-- /.info --> 
                 
               
              <a href="http://www.accuweather.com/us/ny/whitney-point/13862/forecast-details.asp?fday=5" class="bt-more">more</a> 
               
            </div> 
            <!-- /.bg --> 
            </li> 
   
 
   
  </ul> 
   
</div> 
<!-- /.feed-tabs -->
```Am I missing an image file I should have downloaded somewhere ?

### Post by abumaia on 2011-11-05
I just now noticed, as I was trying to integrate the NWS script output into my .conkyrc, that your idea of a forecast is different than mine. This may take some extra time tomorrow to get sorted out. I'm more interested in the hard numbers, not the text. Now I have to figure out how to get the numbers out of the xml file.

The kind of forecast I'm looking to use is like:

```
Saturday     63F - 38F     Breezy
Sunday     50F - 27F     Very Windy
Monday     48F - 27F     Windy
Tuesday     43F - 32F     Sunny

Watches & Warnings     High Wind Watch
```

### Post by BoredOutOfMyMind on 2011-11-05
> **FrankenCub said:**
> I made a huge mess of my conky, was messin with it and payin attention to what my granddaughters were doing and didn't have it backed up lol...Oooops ](*,)
After two weeks of getting it straightened out I decided it was time to go with the Accuweather in the form of Presences' post 403. It looks like it's pulling the data but all I have is...



[Link to post #403]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=11413050&postcount=403")

What directory do you use for the script to run.

Presence uses a hidden directory /.accuweather_conky_USA and if you have conky looking for /accuweather_conky_USA this would explain the blank conky. 

:D

Also run this from terminal

killall conky

conky -c .conky  

You can then see what the error is.  I open gedit with the conky file and after conky opens, click save and that save will refresh conky.  If you are running a terminal you can view the errors. 

I as well overwrote my .conkyrc yesterday.   Thankfully my main conky has a different name and .conkyrc is a working directory.  Pastebin.com is a great resource to save your conky files as well.

### Post by BoredOutOfMyMind on 2011-11-05
> **abumaia said:**
> Ok, I've decided to use the NWS script for forecast data, and data from my station's currdat.lst for current conditions. Only problem is I do not know how to set up the sed to pull a specific piece of data. The current outdoor temp reported by my station is listed as deg_F = "52.0". How do I set up the sed to grab only the data between the quotes? I've already figured out how to get it to read the right line.

Edit: Nevermind, I figured it out after several hours of trial and error: sed -n '43 s/.*"\(.*\)"/\1/p' currdat.lst

Now I just have to be careful which font I use. For some reason Conky is putting that rectangle box character just after the sed data. It does not appear when I send the sed output to a text file, so it has to be Conky's fault.

hmmmm----the "p" at the end is calling for a font is my guess for the error of the box. 

Can you post the line that is giving you problems and someone can help pinpoint what to fix?

### Post by abumaia on 2011-11-05
> **BoredOutOfMyMind said:**
> hmmmm----the "p" at the end is calling for a font is my guess for the error of the box. 

Can you post the line that is giving you problems and someone can help pinpoint what to fix?
The "p" at the end tells sed to print the line since the -n at the beginning suppresses it. Without the -n and the p, sed just reprints the entire file, with no regard for the line number restriction. I just now tested it in terminal.

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-11-05
> **BoredOutOfMyMind said:**
> Teo-

There are errors in the Images1 and Image2 files and directions - International .  The image files will not open for me. 

Directions say - 

The downloads were for Images1 and Images2


Before extracting the archive, you have to join the two pieces together.
```
cat Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images1.tar.gz Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images2.tar.gz > Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images.tar.gz
```
You then extract the final, joined archive.

### Post by Sector11 on 2011-11-05
It took a bit - I type s-l-o-w, and I had to figure out what "p" value from what file meant what and where to put it.  But I did it, trimmed it down in size and eliminated the "text" for the forecast part as that changes a few times throughout the day anyway, add to the fact a picture says a thousand words the text can't match that.

This is it Under Construction:
[CENTER][[IMG]http://thumbnails61.imagebam.com/15770/997f9f157693028.jpg[/IMG]](http://www.imagebam.com/image/997f9f157693028)[/CENTER]

And the finished conky in action:
[CENTER][[IMG]http://thumbnails65.imagebam.com/15770/5d6ee6157693288.jpg[/IMG]](http://www.imagebam.com/image/5d6ee6157693288)[/CENTER]

**The conky:**
```
## killall conky && conky -c ~/Conky/TBG/conkyrc_acc_ii &

 ###  Begin Window Settings  ##################################################
 # Create own window instead of using desktop (required in nautilus)
own_window yes
own_window_type override
# own_window_type normal - creates conky in a window.
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_hints below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
# own_window_colour brown
own_window_class Conky
own_window_title Accuweather Bs As

 ### ARGB can be used for real transparency
 ### NOTE that a composite manager is required for real transparency.
 ### This option will not work as desired (in most cases) in conjunction with
 ### 'own_window_type override'
# own_window_argb_visual yes
 ### When ARGB visuals are enabled, this use this to modify the alpha value
 ### Valid range is 0-255, where 0 is 0% opacity, and 255 is 100% opacity. 
#own_window_argb_value 0

minimum_size 350 0	## width, height
maximum_width 350	## width - usually a good idea to equal minimum width.

gap_x 10	## left &right
gap_y 10	## up & down

alignment top_left
 ####################################################  End Window Settings  ###
 ###  Font Settings  ##########################################################
 # Use Xft (anti-aliased font and stuff) 
use_xft yes
xftfont monospace:bold:size=10
 # Alpha of Xft font. Must be a value at or between 1 and 0 ###
xftalpha 0
 # Force UTF8? requires XFT ###
override_utf8_locale yes

draw_shades no
draw_outline no
uppercase no

draw_outline no # amplifies text if yes
 ######################################################  End Font Settings  ###
 ###  Color Settings  #########################################################
default_shade_color grey
default_outline_color black

default_color DCDCDC #Gainsboro
color0 DAA520 #Goldenrod  FFFFF0 #Ivory
color1 778899 #LightSlateGrey
color2 FF8C00 #Darkorange
color3 7FFF00 #Chartreuse
color4 FFA07A #LightSalmon
color5 FFDEAD #NavajoWhite
color6 00BFFF #DeepSkyBlue
color7 00FFFF #Cyan 48D1CC #MediumTurquoise
color8 FFFF00 #Yellow
color9 FF0000 #Red
 #####################################################  End Color Settings  ###
 ###  Borders Section  ########################################################
draw_borders no
# Stippled borders?
stippled_borders 0
# border margins
border_inner_margin 5
# border width
border_width 0
 #####################################################  End Borders Secton  ###


 # Use the Xdbe extension? (eliminates flicker)
 # It is highly recommended to use own window with this one so
 # double buffer won't be so big
double_buffer yes

 # Adds spaces around certain objects to stop them
 # from moving other things around.
 # only works with mono fonts
use_spacer right
 # Arguments are left, right, and none (default)

 # Size of the standard text buffer (default is 256 bytes).
 # Will allow for more text display per variable.
text_buffer_size 4096 #256

 # Sets bars size even for bars that do not hace that function
 # IE: execbar and execibar
default_bar_size 335 9

 # 0 makes Conky run forever
total_run_times 0
cpu_avg_samples 1
net_avg_samples 1
no_buffers yes

 # Defaults to 4MiB
 # Set to 0 to disable the image cache
 # Increase this value if you use $image lots
imlib_cache_size 0

update_interval 3

# lua_load ~/Conky/LUA/draw-bg.lua
# lua_draw_hook_pre draw_bg

TEXT
${texeci 500 bash /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/acc_int_images}${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/cc.png -p 0,0 -s 180x108}${goto 200}${color7}Now:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond}°\
${goto 290}${color7}FL:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '4p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond}°
${voffset 5}${goto 200}${color7}  Wind:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond}
${goto 200}${color7}   Hum:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '6p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond}
${goto 200}${color7}Dew Pt:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '7p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond}°
${goto 200}${color7}   Bar:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '8p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond}
${goto 200}${color7}   and${color}  ${execpi 600 sed -n '9p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond}
${goto 200}${color7}   Vis:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '10p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond}
${voffset 5}     ${color7}Sunrise:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '11p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond}\
${goto 200}${color7}Sunset:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '12p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond}
${voffset 5}${font monospace:size=15}${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/curr_cond|fold -s -w 24}${font}
${alignc}${color6}${cpubar 1,70}${color} Forcasted Weather: Today ${color6}${cpubar 1,70}${color}
${color7}Today:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '4p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°${goto 100}${color7}FL:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°${goto 175}${color1}Tonight:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '39p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°${goto 290}${color1}FL:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '40p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/1.png -p 30,200 -s 90x54}
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/36.png -p 200,200 -s 90x54}



  ${color7}Today:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages_curr|fold -s -w 44}
${color1}Tonight:${color} ${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/messages_curr|fold -s -w 44}
${color6}${hr 2}${color}${font monospace:bold:size=8}
${color6}${execpi 600 sed -n '7p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '9p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
 ${color7}/${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '10p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
${goto 131}${color6}${execpi 600 sed -n '12p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '14p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
 ${color7}/${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '15p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}° \
${goto 255}${color6}${execpi 600 sed -n '17p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '19p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
 ${color7}/${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '20p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/6.png -p 15,325 -s 72x43}\
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/11.png -p 135,325 -s 72x43}\
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/16.png -p 260,325 -s 72x43}\




${color1}${hr}${color}
${color1}${execpi 600 sed -n '42p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '44p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
 ${color7}/${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '45p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
${goto 131}${color1}${execpi 600 sed -n '47p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '49p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
 ${color7}/${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '50p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}° \
${goto 255}${color1}${execpi 600 sed -n '52p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '54p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
 ${color7}/${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '55p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/41.png -p 15,405 -s 72x43}\
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/46.png -p 135,405 -s 72x43}\
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/51.png -p 260,405 -s 72x43}



${color6}${hr 2}${color}
${color6}${execpi 600 sed -n '22p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '24p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
 ${color7}/${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '25p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
${goto 131}${color6}${execpi 600 sed -n '27p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '29p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
 ${color7}/${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '30p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}° \
${goto 255}${color6}${execpi 600 sed -n '32p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '34p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
 ${color7}/${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '35p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/21.png -p 15,480 -s 72x43}\
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/26.png -p 135,480 -s 72x43}\
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/31.png -p 260,480 -s 72x43}\




${color1}${hr}${color}
${color1}${execpi 600 sed -n '57p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '59p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
 ${color7}/${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '60p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
${goto 131}${color1}${execpi 600 sed -n '62p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '64p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
 ${color7}/${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '65p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}° \
${goto 255}${color1}${execpi 600 sed -n '67p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '69p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°\
 ${color7}/${color}\
 ${execpi 600 sed -n '70p' /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/week}°
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/56.png -p 15,560 -s 72x43}\
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/61.png -p 135,560 -s 72x43}\
${image /home/sector11/Conky/TBG/66.png -p 260,560 -s 72x43}



${color6}${hr 2}${color}
```

### Post by abumaia on 2011-11-05
For the NWS script, is there any way we can group the "period-name" data and the "<value>" data by which section they are in? I noticed around 1530 another day is added to the end of the list, and its associated data throws off the line count. If I could get the data grouped by section, that would eliminate the line-count problem.

Edit: Nevermind on this one too. I've solved my problem by ditching the NWS script and instead signing up for the WUnderground API. This lets me access an xml feed with all the data I need, even in the US, for free (for now).

[IMG]http://dl.dropbox.com/u/8725574/Screenshot%20at%202011-11-05%2018%3A22%3A05.png[/IMG]

### Post by FrankenCub on 2011-11-06
> **BoredOutOfMyMind said:**
> [Link to post #403]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=11413050&postcount=403")

What directory do you use for the script to run.

Presence uses a hidden directory /.accuweather_conky_USA and if you have conky looking for /accuweather_conky_USA this would explain the blank conky. 

:D

Also run this from terminal

killall conky

conky -c .conky  

You can then see what the error is.  I open gedit with the conky file and after conky opens, click save and that save will refresh conky.  If you are running a terminal you can view the errors. 

I as well overwrote my .conkyrc yesterday.   Thankfully my main conky has a different name and .conkyrc is a working directory.  Pastebin.com is a great resource to save your conky files as well.

I did make the changes to make it all visible when I first started knowing that would be an issue :)
The extended forecast temps have been working, after correcting a spelling mistake I now have the images there.

I am still having problems with the current info and sun/moon info. In terminal I get
```
sed: can't read /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current: No such file or directory

```

I have info pulled into a current_raw directory, when I change it so it looks for the info in that directory I get a jumbled mess of text in the current section of the conky. I'm sure that's where the problem is, but don't understand how to fix it lol.

My main conky is conkyrc, this weather one is conkyrc2

<div class="panel-body-content"> 
  <h3>Today's <strong>Aches &amp; Pains</strong> Tip</h3> 
  <ul> 
    <li> 
      <blockquote><span>Sitting upright at your desk lessens muscle tension and improves oxygen flow and breathing.</span></blockquote> 
    </li> 
  </ul> 
   
</div>

<script>
$('#offers').each(function() {
    var bt = $(this);
    bt.find('.panel-body-content').each(function(i) {
        $(this).click(function(event) {
            try{_gaq.push(['_trackEvent','Sponsored Offers',acmOffersTitles[i],'SO_Click' ]);}catch(e){}
        });
    });
})
.find("p:first")
.bind("grow.afterupdate", function (event) {
    $(this).closest("#offers").hide().show();
})
.grow({ others: 'p:not(:first)', inClosest: '#offers' });
</script>

function onTemplateLoaded(experienceID) { 
    brightcove_adc.player = brightcove.getExperience(experienceID); 
    var adModule = brightcove_adc.player.getModule(APIModules.ADVERTISING); 
    var videoPlayerModule = brightcove_adc.player.getModule(APIModules.VIDEO_PLAYER); 
    var experienceModule = brightcove_adc.player.getModule(APIModules.EXPERIENCE); 
    var menuModule = brightcove_adc.player.getModule(APIModules.MENU); 
    experienceModule.addEventListener(BCExperienceEvent.CONTENT_LOAD, brightcove_adc.onContentLoad); 
    videoPlayerModule.addEventListener("videoLoad", brightcove_adc.onVideoLoad); 
    videoPlayerModule.addEventListener(BCMediaEvent.COMPLETE, adc_video_overlay.onMediaComplete); 
    adModule.enableExternalAds(true); 
    adModule.addEventListener(BCAdvertisingEvent.EXTERNAL_AD, brightcove_adc.onExternalAd); 
    adModule.addEventListener(BCAdvertisingEvent.AD_COMPLETE, brightcove_adc.onAdComplete); 
    adModule.addEventListener(BCAdvertisingEvent.AD_CLICK, brightcove_adc.onVideoClick); 
    menuModule.addEventListener(BCMenuEvent.OVERLAY_MENU_PLAY_CLICK,adc_video_overlay.replayClicked); 
    adc_video_overlay.displayAds(); 
}; 
</script>

### Post by BoredOutOfMyMind on 2011-11-06
> **FrankenCub said:**
> I gotta be missing something and getting to tired to see it right now. Could it be that the missing "Current" info isn't being received correctly ? It looks kinda odd to me for some reason...


~/accuweather_conky_USA/accuw_USA_script

```
#!/bin/bash

#function: test_image
test_image () {
	case $1 in
	 c|d|f)
	   echo e
	 ;;
	 fl|sn)
	   echo q
	 ;;
	 cl)
	   echo A
	 ;;
	 pc)
	   echo C
	 ;;
	 co)
	   echo E
	 ;;
	 fr|r)
	   echo i
	 ;;
	 h|ic)
	   echo b
	 ;;
	 ho)
	   echo 5
	 ;;
	 i)
	   echo r
	 ;;
	 mcl)
	   echo B
	 ;;
	 mc)
	   echo c
	 ;;
	 mcfl|mcsn|psfl)
	   echo o
	 ;;
	 mcs)
	   echo g
	 ;;
	 pcs)
	   echo G
	 ;;
	 mct|psut)
	   echo k
	 ;;
	 pct)
	   echo K
	 ;;
	 msu|psu)
	   echo b
	 ;;
	 psus)
	   echo g
	 ;;
	 s)
	   echo h
	 ;;
	 rsn|sl)
	   echo x
	 ;;
	 su)
	   echo a
	 ;;
	 t)
	   echo m
	 ;;
	 w)
	   echo 6
	 ;;
	esac
} 

wget -O /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/raw http://www.accuweather.com/us/ny/whitney-point/13862/forecast-current-conditions.asp
cp /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/raw /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw
sed -i '/feed-tabs/,/\/.feed-tabs/!d' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/raw

#file: days
grep '#' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/raw > /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/days
sed -i 's/<\|>/\n/g' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/days
sed -i '/Today\|Tonight\|Overnight\|Tomorrow\|Monday\|Tuesday\|Wednesday\|Thursday\|Friday\|Saturday\|Sunday/!d' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/days

#file: messages
grep 'cond' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/raw > /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/messages
sed -i 's/<\|>/\n/g' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/messages
sed -i '/span class="cond"\|\/span/d' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/messages

#file: temperatures
grep 'temp' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/raw > /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures
sed -i 's/"temp">\|<span>&\|&\|<\/span>\ /\n/g' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures
sed -i '/<strong class=\|deg;<\/span><\/strong><span class="low"><span>Lo\|deg;<\/span>/d' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures
day1=$(sed -n '1p' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/days)
if [[ $day1 == Overnight || $day1 == Tonight ]]; then
   sed -i 1i"-" /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/temperatures
fi

#file: images
grep 'icon' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/raw > /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/images
sed -i 's/\ i-alarm//g' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/images
sed -i 's/\ i-\|"></\n/g' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/images
sed -i '/<div class="icon\|\/div>/d' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/images
for (( i=1; i<=5; i++ ))
  do
   test_number=$(sed -n "${i}p" /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/images)
   test_image $test_number >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/images

  done
messag1=$(sed -n '1p' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/days)
if [[ $messag1 == Tonight || $messag1 == Overnight ]]; then
   imag1=$(sed -n '1p' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/images)
   case $imag1 in
    h|ic)
      sed -i '6s/b/B/' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    mc)
      sed -i '6s/c/C/' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    mcfl|mcsn)
      sed -i '6s/o/O/' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    mcs)
      sed -i '6s/g/G/' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    mct)
      sed -i '6s/k/K/' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/images
    ;;
    esac
fi


#current conditions
sed -i '/#detail-tab-buttons/,/\/#panel-main/!d' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw

#file: current
grep 'realfeel' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw > /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Humidity:' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Pressure:' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Cloud Cover:' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'UV Index:' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Dew Point:' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Precipitation' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'Visibility:' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep '"more-info"' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'position:absolute;' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'start' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep 'finish' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
grep '="icon' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current_raw >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current

sed -i 's/[ ]*<div class="icon i-//' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current

sed -i -e 's/[ ]*<div[a-zA-Z0-9 =":;-]*>//g' -e 's/[ ]*<span[a-zA-Z0-9 =":;-]*>//g' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/<\/span><\/span>/<\/span>/g' -e 's/<\/span>/\n/g' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/[ ]*<li>[a-zA-Z0-9 ()]*: <strong>//g' -e 's/<\/strong><\/li>//g' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/&nbsp;/ /g' -e 's/&deg;//g' -e 's/<\/div>//g' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current

sed -i -e 's/RealFeel&reg; //' -e 's/ <img src="\/adc2010\/images\/icons-wind\///' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/.gif" width="260" height="138" alt="" \/>//' -e 's/">//' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current

sed -i -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) in/\1/g' -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) Miles/\1/g' -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) mph/\1/g' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
sed -i -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) F/\1/g' -e 's/\([0-9.]*\) %/\1/g' -e 's/\([0-9.]*\)%/\1/g' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current


#If you want AM and PM in uppercase, comment this line out
sed -i -e 's/ AM/am/g' -e 's/ PM/pm/g' /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current

test_number=$(sed -n "22p" /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current)
test_image $test_number >> /home/brendan/accuweather_conky_USA/current
```

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-11-06
> **mgalvezb said:**
> Hi guys.. I have a similar problem like user [Phyllinux]("http://ubuntuforums.org/member.php?u=1092517") (conky accuwather int images) dont show images.
I probe change - for + , but I dont have images...

This is my url:
[http://www.accuweather.com/es-mx/cl/region-metropolitana/santiago/quick-look.aspx](http://www.accuweather.com/es-mx/cl/region-metropolitana/santiago/quick-look.aspx) 

Any idea?

Thanks

Change this line
```
egrep -i 'monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday|sunday|&deg|lblDesc|imgIcon' /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images/week_raw > /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images/week
```
to
```
egrep -i 'lunes|martes|miércoles|jueves|viernes|sábado|domingo|&deg|lblDesc|imgIcon' /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images/week_raw > /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images/week
```
and this line
```
sed -i -e 's/Monday/MONDAY/' -e 's/Tuesday/TUESDAY/' -e 's/Wednesday/WEDNESDAY/' -e 's/Thursday/THURSDAY/' -e 's/Friday/FRIDAY/' -e 's/Saturday/SATURDAY/' -e 's/Sunday/SUNDAY/' /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images/week
```
to
```
sed -i -e 's/lunes/LUNES/' -e 's/martes/MARTES/' -e 's/miércoles/MIERCOLES/' -e 's/jueves/JUEVES/' -e 's/viernes/VIERNES/' -e 's/sábado/SABADO/' -e 's/domingo/DOMINGO/' /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_Int_Images/week
```

If you want to, you can open the .conkyrc_int_im file and replace the strings "TEMP:", "WIND:", "HUM:", etc. with their equivalent in spanish.

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-11-06
_HOW I GOT THE US IMAGES FROM ACCUWEATHER_

I owe this explanation to Sector11 - see from [here]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=11407007#post11407007") onwards.

The images from the international forecast were easy to get. But what about the images from the us weather? If you look into the raw files generated by the us scripts, the only reference to images is something like
```
icon i-pc
```
The heck is that?

To the rescue came the best browser out there: Opera (What? You're not using it? Start right NOW).

If you go to Accuweather, select a place to see its forecast and then click on a day

[[IMG]http://i.imgur.com/DcRETs.jpg[/IMG]]("http://i.imgur.com/DcRET.jpg")

you can right click the page with Opera and select "Inspect element".
Clicking on an icon at the forecast, some interesting things come up:

[[IMG]http://i.imgur.com/X08AZs.jpg[/IMG]]("http://i.imgur.com/X08AZ.jpg")

I can see the web address of the icon. Oh wait... is that a css style? Let's click on it:

[[IMG]http://i.imgur.com/EEBBUs.jpg[/IMG]]("http://i.imgur.com/EEBBU.jpg")

Aha!!! Copying this link on my address bar I get redirected to this:

[[IMG]http://i.imgur.com/I9Lfns.jpg[/IMG]]("http://i.imgur.com/I9Lfn.jpg")

Ctrl+A to select all the text, Ctrl+C to copy it and straight to Geany for a Ctrl+V.
Then Ctrl+H for a find and replace: url to be replaced by \n ("Use regular expressions"). The result, after a few more replacements, is this:

```
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/su-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/msu-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/psu-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/ic-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/h-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/mc-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/c-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/d-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/f-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/s-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/mcs-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/psus-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/t-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/mct-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/psut-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/r-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/fl-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/mcfl-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/psfl-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/sn-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/mcsn-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/i-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/sl-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/fr-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/rsn-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/w-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/ho-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-day/co-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-night/cl-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-night/w-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-night/mcl-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-night/pc-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-night/ic-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-night/h-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-night/mc-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-night/pcs-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-night/mcs-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-night/pct-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-night/mct-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-night/mcfl-s.png
http://vortex.accuweather.com/adc2010/images/icons-night/mcsn-s.png
```
These are the images used by accuweather for their us forecast.

Furthermore, if you replace the -s in the web addresses with -l, you'll get the large size icons, which are the icons I've finally used in the scripts.

Now go and use Opera!!!!!:lolflag:
