### Post by Sector11 on 2011-11-22
You guys are too fast for me ... here's what I was thinking:

All of what follows are just suggestions:

You are opening fonts and not closing them.  Which means spacing (vertical) is getting messed up.

See this: [Spacing in Conky](http://conky.pitstop.free.fr/wiki/index.php5?title=Spacing_in_Conky_%28en%29) and [goto/offset/voffset](http://conky.pitstop.free.fr/wiki/index.php5?title=Goto/offset/voffset_%28en%29)

You have:

```
${font DroidSans:size=11}${color3}Currently:${goto 240}${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/images}
${font DroidSans:size=11}blah blah
```

The second line is virtically spaced according to "conkyweather:size=40" which is a lot bigger than "DroidSans:size=11"

Also if "DroidSans:size=11" is not your "default" font this is what you need:

```
${voffset 6}${font DroidSans:bold:size=12}${color4}BURY WEATHER${font}${offset 8}${color8}${voffset -2}${hr 2}${execi 600 bash /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/accuw_script}

${font DroidSans:size=11}${color3}Currently:${font}${goto 240}${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/images}${font}
${font DroidSans:size=11}${voffset -70}${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -s -w30}
${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C

${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/days}:${goto 240}${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/images}${font}
${font DroidSans:size=11}${voffset -70}${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -s -w30}
High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C

${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/days}:
${goto 240}${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '7p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/images}${font}
${font DroidSans:size=11}${voffset -90}${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -s -w30}
High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '4p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C

${font DroidSans:size=11}${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/days}:
${goto 240}${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '8p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/images}${font}
${font DroidSans:size=11}${voffset -90}${execpi 600 sed -n '7p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -s -w30}
High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '6p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '7p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C
```


If DroidSans:size=11 is you're default font this is what you need:

```
${voffset 6}${font DroidSans:bold:size=12}${color4}BURY WEATHER${font}${offset 8}${color8}${voffset -2}${hr 2}${execi 600 bash /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/accuw_script}

${color3}Currently:${font}${goto 240}${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '5p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/images}${font}
${voffset -70}${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -s -w30}
${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C

${execpi 600 sed -n '1p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/days}:${goto 240}${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '6p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/images}${font}
${voffset -70}${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -s -w30}
High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '2p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C

${execpi 600 sed -n '3p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/days}:
${goto 240}${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '7p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/images}${font}
${voffset -90}${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -s -w30}
High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '4p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C

${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/days}:
${goto 240}${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '8p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/images}${font}
${voffset -90}${execpi 600 sed -n '7p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -s -w30}
High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '6p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '7p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C
```

Also you are calling the LARGE weather font and then placing the data, may I suggest:

```
${font DroidSans:size=11}${execpi 600 sed -n '5p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/days}:
${execpi 600 sed -n '7p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/messages|fold -s -w30}
High: ${execpi 600 sed -n '6p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C  Low: ${execpi 600 sed -n '7p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/temperatures}C
${goto 240}${voffset -xx}${font conkyweather:size=40}${execi 600  sed -n '8p' /home/mdcomm/accuweather_conky/images}${font}
```

You only need "${font DroidSans:size=11}" once and you are only offsetting the weather font.

Any of these solutions are going to mean that your ${voffset} values will change, but they should be uniform for each section.

I use this method and when I want day two I cut past day one below it and just change these parts: **'7p'** - works every time.

EDIT: **{sigh - YUP really too fast - I have to learn to type faster}**

### Post by Sector11 on 2011-11-22
> **Quackers said:**
> Thanks Sector11.  You need to close fonts after opening them? I have a lot to learn :-)
This comes by copying other conkyrc's then trying to figure out what's going on :-)
Thanks for the links - I'll investigate :-)

If you open a "font:size=60" and do a second line without closing it, the space between lines is much bigger than a "font:size=20"
Hi there = 60 and 20 ... see below.

In conky once a font is open it determines the space between lines and characters on a line until it is closed or changed.

The "special" font here is the weather-icon - that should be opened, used and closed to get back to business.

The font and size you use "most" should be the default font.

I see you use ${offset} as well - be careful with that one, never use it after a conky command that gives different character length responses unless you are willing to see your results move around a bit.

```
Now: ${temperature_command}°F${offset 20}HI
```
Can give you things like
```
Now: 2°F   HI
Now: -25°F   HI
Now: 75°F   HI
Now: 104°F   HI
```

All of the above are just opinions - please take them that way.

Another good piece of advice is to use the {} for ALL commands, there is a time (forget which actually - Mark of conkyForecast knows) that if certain conky commands are not in {} it messes up the conky and it has to do with going online for stuff.  So, years ago, I got into the habit of doing that.  BTW, I had the messed up conky and Mark explained to me why, you'd think I could remember. :D

---

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-11-22
> **Quackers said:**
> 
The last font opened is used for everything after unless you close that font by opening a new font. 

You don't have to open a new font; everything not inside conky font tags, has the default conky font, described in the conkyrc line before the TEXT section (something like "xftfont Arial:size=9").

### Post by Sector11 on 2011-11-22
> **Quackers said:**
> I wasn't aware I had a default font :-)

Above TEXT you have a line that starts like this:

```
font blah blah
```
or
```
xftfont font_name with options
```

ie:
```
font Anonymous Pro:size=8
xftfont Anonymous Pro:bold:size=8
xftfont Anonymous Pro:italics:size=8
xftfont Anonymous Pro:bold:italics:size=8
```

Any time, under TEXT, you start a different font or the same font with different options that font says active until one of two things happens:

[LIST=1]
[*]you close it: ${font} at which point the default font set above TEXT will become the font used, or
[*]you change the font: ${font monospace:bold:size=15}
[/LIST]

### Post by BoredOutOfMyMind on 2011-11-22
> **davesbrain said:**
> I can really hug you guys about now.  Repackaged script work like a charm.:D
My only dilemma now is that it's too long.  I would like to move the weather section up and to the right of all the other stuff(so it would be a square instead of a long rectangle).  Or maybe give it it's own conky and launch both simultaneously via .sh.

Word to the wise from one who has lost .conkyrc.  Save your conky file as .conkyrc*date*:D
The easiest way is with another conky window. The tougher part would be to play with {goto } and {voffset} variables to display in conky where you want it to. You may also need to learn fold commands and add use_spacer right above the TEXT areas of your conky. 

This beginning sets a conky in a clickable, moveable window. 

```
 ###  Begin Window Settings  ##################################################
 # Create own window instead of using desktop (required in nautilus)
own_window yes
own_window_argb_visual yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_type normal
own_window_hints sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager#undecorated,below,
 own_window_colour brown
own_window_class Conky
own_window_title Weather Temp
```

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-11-23
You had a mistake in your script. Try with this:
```
#!/bin/bash

#function: test_image_day
test_image_day () {
    case $1 in
         su)
           echo 1su
         ;;
         msu)
           echo 2msu
         ;;
         psu)
           echo 3psu
         ;;
         ic)
           echo 4ic
         ;;
         h)
           echo 5h
         ;;
         mc)
           echo 6mc
         ;;
         c)
           echo 7c
         ;;
         d)
           echo 8d
         ;;
         f)
           echo 9f
         ;;
         s)
           echo 10s
         ;;
         mcs)
           echo 11mcs
         ;;
         psus)
           echo 12psus
         ;;
         t)
           echo 13t
         ;;
         mct)
           echo 14mct
         ;;
         psut)
           echo 15psut
         ;;
         r)
           echo 16r
         ;;
         fl)
           echo 17fl
         ;;
         mcfl)
           echo 18mcfl
         ;;
         psfl)
           echo 19psfl
         ;;
         sn)
           echo 20sn
         ;;
         mcsn)
           echo 21mcsn
         ;;
         i)
           echo 22i
         ;;
         sl)
           echo 23sl
         ;;
         fr)
           echo 24fr
         ;;
         rsn)
           echo 25rsn
         ;;
         w)
           echo 26w
         ;;
         ho)
           echo 27ho
         ;;
         co)
           echo 28co
         ;;
         cl)
           echo 29cl 
         ;;
         mcl)
           echo 31mcl
         ;;
         pc)
           echo 32pc 
         ;;
         pcs)
           echo 36pcs
         ;;
         pct)
           echo 38pct 
         ;;
        esac
} 

#function: test_image_night
test_image_night () {
    case $1 in
		 su)
           echo 1su
         ;;
         msu)
           echo 2msu
         ;;
         psu)
           echo 3psu
         ;;
         c)
           echo 7c
         ;;
         d)
           echo 8d
         ;;
         f)
           echo 9f
         ;;
         s)
           echo 10s
         ;;
         psus)
           echo 12psus
         ;;
         t)
           echo 13t
         ;;
         psut)
           echo 15psut
         ;;
         r)
           echo 16r
         ;;
         fl)
           echo 17fl
         ;;
         psfl)
           echo 19psfl
         ;;
         sn)
           echo 20sn
         ;;
         i)
           echo 22i
         ;;
         sl)
           echo 23sl
         ;;
         fr)
           echo 24fr
         ;;
         rsn)
           echo 25rsn
         ;;
         ho)
           echo 27ho
         ;;
         co)
           echo 28co
         ;;
         cl)
           echo 29cl 
         ;;
         w)
           echo 30w
         ;;
         mcl)
           echo 31mcl
         ;;
         pc)
           echo 32pc 
         ;;
         ic)
           echo 33ic
         ;;
         h)
           echo 34h
         ;;
         mc)
           echo 35mc 
         ;;
         pcs)
           echo 36pcs
         ;;
         mcs)
           echo 37mcs 
         ;;
         pct)
           echo 38pct 
         ;;
         mct)
           echo 39mct 
         ;;
         mcfl)
           echo 40mcfl 
         ;;
         mcsn)
           echo 41mcsn 
         ;;
        esac
}

#put your Accuweather address here
address="http://www.accuweather.com/en/us/mansfield-oh/44902/weather-forecast/330112"

loc_id=$(echo $address|sed 's/\/weather-forecast.*$//'|sed 's/^.*\///')
last_number=$(echo $address|sed 's/^.*\///')

kill -STOP $(pidof conky)
killall wget
rm ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/*.png

#current conditions
curr_addr="$(echo $address|sed 's/weather-forecast.*$//')"current-weather/"$loc_id"us?day=1
wget -O ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond_raw "$curr_addr"
sed -i '/detail-now/,/Moonrise/!d' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond_raw
egrep -i '"cond"|Humidity|Pressure|Cloud Cover|UV Index|Dew Point|Amount of Precipitation|Visibility|AM|PM|mph|icons-wind|detail-tab-panel|icon i-' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond_raw > ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/^.*icon i-//g' -e 's/^.*tab-panel //g' -e 's/^.*start">//g' -e 's/^.*finish">//g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond
sed -i 's/^.*<li>//g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/<strong>//g' -e 's/<\/strong>//g' -e 's/<\/li>//g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond
sed -i -e '1d' -e 's/^.*center;">//g' -e 's/^.*icons-wind\///g' -e 's/\.gif.*$//g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/^.*"cond">//g' -e 's/temp">/\n/g' -e 's/realfeel">/\n/g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/">.*$//g' -e 's/<\/span>.*$//g' -e 's/<span>.*$//g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/&deg;//g' -e 's/&nbsp;//g' -e 's/RealFeel&reg; //g' -e 's/<\/div>.*$//g' -e '15d' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/Humidity: //g' -e 's/Pressure: //g' -e 's/Cloud Cover: //g' -e 's/UV Index: //g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/Dew Point: //g' -e 's/Amount of Precipitation(1 Hr): //g' -e 's/Visibility: //g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond
sed -i 's/ i-alarm.*$//g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond
time=$(sed -n 1p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond)
image=$(sed -n 2p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond)
if [[ $time == day ]]; then
    sed -i 2s/$image/$(test_image_day $image)/ ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond
elif [[ $time == night ]]; then
    sed -i 2s/$image/$(test_image_night $image)/ ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond
fi
cp ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/Forecast_Images/$(sed -n 2p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/curr_cond).png ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/cc.png

#today - tonight - first 4 days
addr1="$(echo $address|sed 's/weather-forecast.*$//')"daily-weather-forecast/"$last_number"
wget -O ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton_raw "$addr1"
sed -i '/day=1/,/<h4>Night<\/h4>/!d' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton_raw 
egrep -i 'h3|"cond"|temp|icon i-|RealFeel' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton_raw > ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton
sed -i -e '1s/^.*"#">//' -e '1s/<.*$//' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton
now=$(sed -n 1p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton)
if [[ $now == Tonight || $now == Overnight ]]; then
	sed -i '1s/night/night\n/' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton
fi
sed -i -e 's/^.*icon i-//g' -e 's/^.*"cond">//g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton
sed -i -e '1,21s/^.*"temp">//g' -e 's/^.*"#">//g' -e 's/Lo<\/span> /\n/' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton
sed -i -e '27s/<span>&deg;/\n/' -e 's/<\/a>.*$//g' -e 's/"><.*$//g' -e 's/<\/span><\/span> <span class="realfeel">RealFeel&reg; //g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton
sed -i -e 's/^ *//g' -e '22s/<span>&deg;/\n/' -e 's/<span>.*$//g' -e 's/<\/span>.*"temp">/\n/' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton
sed -i -e 's/<\/span>.*$//g' -e 's/&deg;.*$//g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton
sed -i -e 's/Tomorrow/TOMORROW/' -e 's/Monday/MONDAY/' -e 's/Tuesday/TUESDAY/' -e 's/Wednesday/WEDNESDAY/' -e 's/Thursday/THURSDAY/' -e 's/Friday/FRIDAY/' -e 's/Saturday/SATURDAY/' -e 's/Sunday/SUNDAY/' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton
sed -i 's/ i-alarm.*$//g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton
for (( i=7; i<=22; i+=5 ))
  do
      image_raw=$(sed -n "${i}"p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton)
      sed -i ${i}s/$image_raw/$(test_image_day $image_raw)/ ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton
      cp ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/Forecast_Images/$(sed -n ${i}p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton).png ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/$i.png
  done
image_raw=$(sed -n 26p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton)
sed -i 26s/$image_raw/$(test_image_day $image_raw)/ ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton
cp ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/Forecast_Images/$(sed -n 26p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton).png ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/today.png
image_raw=$(sed -n 30p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton)
sed -i 30s/$image_raw/$(test_image_night $image_raw)/ ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton
cp ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/Forecast_Images/$(sed -n 30p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton).png ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tonight.png

#last days
addr2="$addr1"?day=6
echo $addr2
wget -O ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days_raw "$addr2"
sed -i '/day=6/,/<h4>Night<\/h4>/!d' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days_raw 
egrep -i 'h3|"cond"|temp|icon i-|RealFeel' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days_raw > ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days
sed -i -e 's/^.*icon i-//g' -e 's/^.*"cond">//g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days
sed -i -e '1,21s/^.*"temp">//g' -e 's/^.*"#">//g' -e 's/Lo<\/span> /\n/' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days
sed -i -e '27s/<span>&deg;/\n/' -e 's/<\/a>.*$//g' -e 's/"><.*$//g' -e 's/<\/span><\/span> <span class="realfeel">RealFeel&reg; //g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days
sed -i -e 's/^ *//g' -e '22s/<span>&deg;/\n/' -e 's/<span>.*$//g' -e 's/<\/span>.*"temp">/\n/' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days
sed -i -e 's/<\/span>.*$//g' -e 's/&deg;.*$//g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days
sed -i -e 's/Monday/MONDAY/' -e 's/Tuesday/TUESDAY/' -e 's/Wednesday/WEDNESDAY/' -e 's/Thursday/THURSDAY/' -e 's/Friday/FRIDAY/' -e 's/Saturday/SATURDAY/' -e 's/Sunday/SUNDAY/' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days
sed -i 's/ i-alarm.*$//g' ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days
for (( i=2; i<=22; i+=5 ))
  do
      image_raw=$(sed -n "${i}"p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days)
      sed -i ${i}s/$image_raw/$(test_image_day $image_raw)/ ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days
      cp ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/Forecast_Images/$(sed -n ${i}p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days).png ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/N$i.png
  done

#messages - messages_curr
sed -n 27p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton > ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_curr
sed -n 31p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton >> ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_curr
sed -n 8p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton > ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_raw
sed -n 13p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton >> ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_raw
sed -n 18p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton >> ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_raw
sed -n 23p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/tod_ton >> ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_raw
sed -n 3p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days >> ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_raw
sed -n 8p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days >> ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_raw
sed -n 13p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days >> ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_raw
sed -n 18p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days >> ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_raw
sed -n 23p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/last_days >> ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_raw
for (( i=1; i<=4; i++))
	do
		no=$(sed -n ${i}p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_curr | wc -c)
		if (( no<=31 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/$/\n/" ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_curr
			i=$((i+1))
		elif (( no>31 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/^\(.\{31\}\)/\1\n/" ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_curr
			i=$((i+1))
		fi
	done
cat ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_raw | cut -c -40 > ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages
for (( i=1; i<=18; i++))
	do
		no=$(sed -n ${i}p ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages | wc -c)
		if (( no<=21 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/$/\n/" ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages
			i=$((i+1))
		elif (( no>21 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/^\(.\{21\}\)/\1\n/" ~/1e_Accuweather_USA_Images/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages
			i=$((i+1))
		fi
	done

kill -CONT $(pidof conky)
```

### Post by davesbrain on 2011-11-24
Sooooo close.  Probably something stupid.  Big gap between top of image and top of window.:confused:
[ATTACH]207905[/ATTACH]

my conkyrc:
```
own_window yes
#own_window_type desktop
#own_window_transparent yes
#own_window_hints undecorate,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager,below
own_window_title RADAR

# Use double buffering (reduces flicker, may not work for everyone)
double_buffer yes

# Draw borders around text
draw_borders no

# Text alignment, other possible values are commented
#alignment top_left
alignment top_right
#alignment bottom_left
#alignment bottom_right

lua_load /home/davejr/.conky/Radar/radartest.lua
lua_draw_hook_pre radar

TEXT

```

And my radartest.lua
```
--[[
call 
lua_load /home/davejr/.conky/Radar/radartest.lua
lua_draw_hook_pre radar
]]

require 'cairo'
require 'imlib2'
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
function string:split(delimiter)
local result = { }
local from  = 1
local delim_from, delim_to = string.find( self, delimiter, from  )
while delim_from do
table.insert( result, string.sub( self, from , delim_from-1 ) )
from  = delim_to + 1
delim_from, delim_to = string.find( self, delimiter, from  )
end
table.insert( result, string.sub( self, from  ) )
return result
end
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

function conky_radar()
if conky_window == nil then return end
local cs = cairo_xlib_surface_create(conky_window.display, conky_window.drawable, conky_window.visual, conky_window.width, conky_window.height)
cr = cairo_create(cs)
local updates=tonumber(conky_parse('${updates}'))
if updates>5 then
--#########################################################################################################
--#########################################################################################################
file="/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_Topo_Short.jpg"
image(300,400,0,0,file)

interval=300
timer=(updates % interval)+1
if timer==1 or updates==10 then
if timer==1 or updates==10 then
os.execute("rm /home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_N0R_0.gif")
os.execute("rm /home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_N0R_Legend_0.gif")
os.execute("rm /home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_Warnings_0.gif")

os.execute("wget --directory-prefix=/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/ http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/RadarImg/N0R/FTG_N0R_0.gif")
os.execute("wget --directory-prefix=/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/ http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/Legend/N0R/FTG_N0R_Legend_0.gif")
os.execute("wget --directory-prefix=/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/ http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/Warnings/Short/FTG_Warnings_0.gif")
end
end
file="/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_N0R_0.gif"
image(300,400,0,0,file)

file="/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_County_Short.gif"
image(300,400,0,0,file)
file="/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_Highways_Short.gif"
image(300,400,0,0,file)

file="/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_N0R_Legend_0.gif"
image(300,400,0,0,file)

file="/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_Warnings_0.gif"
image(300,400,0,0,file)

end-- if updates>5
cairo_destroy(cr)
cairo_surface_destroy(cs)
cr=nil
end-- end main function

function image(center_x,center_y,w,h,file)
show = imlib_load_image(file)
if show == nil then return end
imlib_context_set_image(show)
if tonumber(w)==0 then 
width=imlib_image_get_width() 
else
width=tonumber(w)
end
if tonumber(h)==0 then 
height=imlib_image_get_height() 
else
height=tonumber(h)
end
iacross=center_x-(width/2)
idown=center_y-(height/2)
imlib_context_set_image(show)
scaled=imlib_create_cropped_scaled_image(0, 0, imlib_image_get_width(), imlib_image_get_height(), width, height)
imlib_free_image()
imlib_context_set_image(scaled)
imlib_render_image_on_drawable(iacross, idown)
imlib_free_image()
end
```

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-11-25
Change these lines 

```
for (( i=1; i<=4; i++))
	do
		no=$(sed -n ${i}p /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_curr|wc -c)
		if (( no<=31 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/$/\n/" /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_curr
			i=$((i+1))
		elif (( no>31 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/^\(.\{31\}\)/\1\n/" /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_curr
			i=$((i+1))
		fi
	done
cat /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_raw| cut -c -40 > /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages
for (( i=1; i<=18; i++))
	do
		no=$(sed -n ${i}p /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages|wc -c)
		if (( no<=21 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/$/\n/" /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages
			i=$((i+1))
		elif (( no>21 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/^\(.\{21\}\)/\1\n/" /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages
			i=$((i+1))
		fi
	done
```

at the end of the script according to your liking. The today and tonight messages are folded to 30 characters. If you want to make it, say 20, change 31 to 21 in the first for loop.
The days' messages are folded to 20 characters. If you want to make it 15, for example, change 21 to 16 in the second loop.

PS: I'm waiting for feedback and the raw files for the Accuweather USA missing conditions problem.

# Create own window instead of using desktop (required in nautilus)

And the script:
```
#!/bin/bash

#function: test_image_day
test_image_day () {
    case $1 in
         su)
           echo 1su
         ;;
         msu)
           echo 2msu
         ;;
         psu)
           echo 3psu
         ;;
         ic)
           echo 4ic
         ;;
         h)
           echo 5h
         ;;
         mc)
           echo 6mc
         ;;
         c)
           echo 7c
         ;;
         d)
           echo 8d
         ;;
         f)
           echo 9f
         ;;
         s)
           echo 10s
         ;;
         mcs)
           echo 11mcs
         ;;
         psus)
           echo 12psus
         ;;
         t)
           echo 13t
         ;;
         mct)
           echo 14mct
         ;;
         psut)
           echo 15psut
         ;;
         r)
           echo 16r
         ;;
         fl)
           echo 17fl
         ;;
         mcfl)
           echo 18mcfl
         ;;
         psfl)
           echo 19psfl
         ;;
         sn)
           echo 20sn
         ;;
         mcsn)
           echo 21mcsn
         ;;
         i)
           echo 22i
         ;;
         sl)
           echo 23sl
         ;;
         fr)
           echo 24fr
         ;;
         rsn)
           echo 25rsn
         ;;
         w)
           echo 26w
         ;;
         ho)
           echo 27ho
         ;;
         co)
           echo 28co
         ;;
         cl)
           echo 29cl 
         ;;
         mcl)
           echo 31mcl
         ;;
         pc)
           echo 32pc 
         ;;
         pcs)
           echo 36pcs
         ;;
         pct)
           echo 38pct 
         ;;
        esac
} 

#function: test_image_night
test_image_night () {
    case $1 in
		 su)
           echo 1su
         ;;
         msu)
           echo 2msu
         ;;
         psu)
           echo 3psu
         ;;
         c)
           echo 7c
         ;;
         d)
           echo 8d
         ;;
         f)
           echo 9f
         ;;
         s)
           echo 10s
         ;;
         psus)
           echo 12psus
         ;;
         t)
           echo 13t
         ;;
         psut)
           echo 15psut
         ;;
         r)
           echo 16r
         ;;
         fl)
           echo 17fl
         ;;
         psfl)
           echo 19psfl
         ;;
         sn)
           echo 20sn
         ;;
         i)
           echo 22i
         ;;
         sl)
           echo 23sl
         ;;
         fr)
           echo 24fr
         ;;
         rsn)
           echo 25rsn
         ;;
         ho)
           echo 27ho
         ;;
         co)
           echo 28co
         ;;
         cl)
           echo 29cl 
         ;;
         w)
           echo 30w
         ;;
         mcl)
           echo 31mcl
         ;;
         pc)
           echo 32pc 
         ;;
         ic)
           echo 33ic
         ;;
         h)
           echo 34h
         ;;
         mc)
           echo 35mc 
         ;;
         pcs)
           echo 36pcs
         ;;
         mcs)
           echo 37mcs 
         ;;
         pct)
           echo 38pct 
         ;;
         mct)
           echo 39mct 
         ;;
         mcfl)
           echo 40mcfl 
         ;;
         mcsn)
           echo 41mcsn 
         ;;
        esac
}

#put your Accuweather address here
address="http://www.accuweather.com/en/us/aurora-co/80012/weather-forecast/332211"

loc_id=$(echo $address|sed 's/\/weather-forecast.*$//'|sed 's/^.*\///')
last_number=$(echo $address|sed 's/^.*\///')

kill -STOP $(pidof conky)
killall wget
rm ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/*.png

#current conditions
curr_addr="$(echo $address|sed 's/weather-forecast.*$//')"current-weather/"$loc_id"us?day=1
wget -O ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond_raw "$curr_addr"
sed -i '/detail-now/,/Moonrise/!d' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond_raw
egrep -i '"cond"|Humidity|Pressure|Cloud Cover|UV Index|Dew Point|Amount of Precipitation|Visibility|AM|PM|mph|icons-wind|detail-tab-panel|icon i-' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond_raw > ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/^.*icon i-//g' -e 's/^.*tab-panel //g' -e 's/^.*start">//g' -e 's/^.*finish">//g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond
sed -i 's/^.*<li>//g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/<strong>//g' -e 's/<\/strong>//g' -e 's/<\/li>//g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond
sed -i -e '1d' -e 's/^.*center;">//g' -e 's/^.*icons-wind\///g' -e 's/\.gif.*$//g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/^.*"cond">//g' -e 's/temp">/\n/g' -e 's/realfeel">/\n/g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/">.*$//g' -e 's/<\/span>.*$//g' -e 's/<span>.*$//g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/&deg;//g' -e 's/&nbsp;//g' -e 's/RealFeel&reg; //g' -e 's/<\/div>.*$//g' -e '15d' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/Humidity: //g' -e 's/Pressure: //g' -e 's/Cloud Cover: //g' -e 's/UV Index: //g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond
sed -i -e 's/Dew Point: //g' -e 's/Amount of Precipitation(1 Hr): //g' -e 's/Visibility: //g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond
sed -i 's/ i-alarm.*$//g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond
time=$(sed -n 1p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond)
image=$(sed -n 2p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond)
if [[ $time == day ]]; then
    sed -i 2s/$image/$(test_image_day $image)/ ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond
elif [[ $time == night ]]; then
    sed -i 2s/$image/$(test_image_night $image)/ ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond
fi
cp ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/Forecast_Images/$(sed -n 2p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/curr_cond).png ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/cc.png

#today - tonight - first 4 days
addr1="$(echo $address|sed 's/weather-forecast.*$//')"daily-weather-forecast/"$last_number"
wget -O ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton_raw "$addr1"
sed -i '/day=1/,/<h4>Night<\/h4>/!d' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton_raw 
egrep -i 'h3|"cond"|temp|icon i-|RealFeel' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton_raw > ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton
sed -i -e '1s/^.*"#">//' -e '1s/<.*$//' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton
now=$(sed -n 1p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton)
if [[ $now == Tonight || $now == Overnight ]]; then
	sed -i '1s/night/night\n/' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton
fi
sed -i -e 's/^.*icon i-//g' -e 's/^.*"cond">//g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton
sed -i -e '1,21s/^.*"temp">//g' -e 's/^.*"#">//g' -e 's/Lo<\/span> /\n/' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton
sed -i -e '27s/<span>&deg;/\n/' -e 's/<\/a>.*$//g' -e 's/"><.*$//g' -e 's/<\/span><\/span> <span class="realfeel">RealFeel&reg; //g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton
sed -i -e 's/^ *//g' -e '22s/<span>&deg;/\n/' -e 's/<span>.*$//g' -e 's/<\/span>.*"temp">/\n/' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton
sed -i -e 's/<\/span>.*$//g' -e 's/&deg;.*$//g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton
sed -i -e 's/Tomorrow/TOMORROW/' -e 's/Monday/MON./' -e 's/Tuesday/TUES./' -e 's/Wednesday/WED./' -e 's/Thursday/THURS./' -e 's/Friday/FRI./' -e 's/Saturday/SAT./' -e 's/Sunday/SUN./' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton
sed -i 's/ i-alarm.*$//g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton
for (( i=7; i<=22; i+=5 ))
  do
      image_raw=$(sed -n "${i}"p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton)
      sed -i ${i}s/$image_raw/$(test_image_day $image_raw)/ ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton
      cp ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/Forecast_Images/$(sed -n ${i}p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton).png ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/$i.png
  done
image_raw=$(sed -n 26p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton)
sed -i 26s/$image_raw/$(test_image_day $image_raw)/ ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton
cp ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/Forecast_Images/$(sed -n 26p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton).png ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/today.png
image_raw=$(sed -n 30p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton)
sed -i 30s/$image_raw/$(test_image_night $image_raw)/ ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton
cp ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/Forecast_Images/$(sed -n 30p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton).png ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tonight.png

#last days
addr2="$addr1"?day=6
echo $addr2
wget -O ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days_raw "$addr2"
sed -i '/day=6/,/<h4>Night<\/h4>/!d' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days_raw 
egrep -i 'h3|"cond"|temp|icon i-|RealFeel' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days_raw > ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days
sed -i -e 's/^.*icon i-//g' -e 's/^.*"cond">//g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days
sed -i -e '1,21s/^.*"temp">//g' -e 's/^.*"#">//g' -e 's/Lo<\/span> /\n/' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days
sed -i -e '27s/<span>&deg;/\n/' -e 's/<\/a>.*$//g' -e 's/"><.*$//g' -e 's/<\/span><\/span> <span class="realfeel">RealFeel&reg; //g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days
sed -i -e 's/^ *//g' -e '22s/<span>&deg;/\n/' -e 's/<span>.*$//g' -e 's/<\/span>.*"temp">/\n/' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days
sed -i -e 's/<\/span>.*$//g' -e 's/&deg;.*$//g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days
sed -i -e 's/Monday/MON./' -e 's/Tuesday/TUES./' -e 's/Wednesday/WED./' -e 's/Thursday/THURS./' -e 's/Friday/FRI./' -e 's/Saturday/SAT./' -e 's/Sunday/SUN./' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days
sed -i 's/ i-alarm.*$//g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days
for (( i=2; i<=22; i+=5 ))
  do
      image_raw=$(sed -n "${i}"p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days)
      sed -i ${i}s/$image_raw/$(test_image_day $image_raw)/ ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days
      cp ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/Forecast_Images/$(sed -n ${i}p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days).png ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/N$i.png
  done

#messages - messages_curr
sed -n 27p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton > ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_curr
sed -n 31p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton >> ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_curr
sed -n 8p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton > ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_raw
sed -n 13p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton >> ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_raw
sed -n 18p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton >> ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_raw
sed -n 23p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/tod_ton >> ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_raw
sed -n 3p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days >> ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_raw
sed -n 8p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days >> ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_raw
sed -n 13p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days >> ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_raw
sed -n 18p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days >> ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_raw
sed -n 23p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/last_days >> ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_raw
for (( i=1; i<=4; i++))
	do
		no=$(sed -n ${i}p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_curr|wc -c)
		if (( no<=31 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/$/\n/" ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_curr
			i=$((i+1))
		elif (( no>31 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/^\(.\{31\}\)/\1\n/" ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_curr
			i=$((i+1))
		fi
	done
cat ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_raw| cut -c -40 > ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages
for (( i=1; i<=18; i++))
	do
		no=$(sed -n ${i}p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages|wc -c)
		if (( no<=18 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/$/\n/" ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages
			i=$((i+1))
		elif (( no>18 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/^\(.\{21\}\)/\1\n/" ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages
			i=$((i+1))
		fi
	done

kill -CONT $(pidof conky)




```

require 'cairo'
require 'imlib2'
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
function string:split(delimiter)
local result = { }
local from  = 1
local delim_from, delim_to = string.find( self, delimiter, from  )
while delim_from do
table.insert( result, string.sub( self, from , delim_from-1 ) )
from  = delim_to + 1
delim_from, delim_to = string.find( self, delimiter, from  )
end
table.insert( result, string.sub( self, from  ) )
return result
end
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

function conky_radar()
if conky_window == nil then return end
local cs = cairo_xlib_surface_create(conky_window.display, conky_window.drawable, conky_window.visual, conky_window.width, conky_window.height)
cr = cairo_create(cs)
local updates=tonumber(conky_parse('${updates}'))
if updates>5 then

--positioning
pos_x=400
pos_y=279
file="/home/brendan/.conky/Radar/BGM_Topo_Short.jpg"
image(320,283,0,0,file)

interval=200
timer=(updates % interval)+1
if timer==1 or updates==10 then
if timer==1 or updates==10 then
os.execute("rm /home/brendan/.conky/Radar/BGM_N0R_0.gif")
os.execute("rm /home/brendan/.conky/Radar/BGM_N0R_Legend_0.gif")

os.execute("wget --directory-prefix=/home/brendan/.conky/Radar/ http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/RadarImg/N0R/BGM_N0R_0.gif")
os.execute("wget --directory-prefix=/home/brendan/.conky/Radar/ http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/Legend/N0R/BGM_N0R_Legend_0.gif")
os.execute("wget --directory-prefix=/home/brendan/.conky/Radar/ http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/Warnings/Short/BGM_Warnings_0.gif")
end
end

file="/home/brendan/.conky/Radar/BGM_Topo_Short.jpg"
image(330,283,0,0,file)

### Post by davesbrain on 2011-11-26
> davesbrain posted a working conky/radar lua in this post that may show what you need to change.
That one was not aligned properly.



Try this one:

[ATTACH]207976[/ATTACH]
There might be more stuff in the conky than what's needed, but it was carried over from another conky.

Radarconkyrc
```
# Use Xft?
use_xft yes
xftfont Ubuntu:size=10
xftalpha 0.8
text_buffer_size 2048

imlib_cache_size 4096

# Update interval in seconds
update_interval 5

# This is the number of times Conky will update before quitting.
# Set to zero to run forever.
total_run_times 0

# Create own window instead of using desktop (required in nautilus)
own_window yes
#own_window_type desktop
#own_window_transparent yes
#own_window_hints undecorate,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager,below
own_window_title RADAR

# Use double buffering (reduces flicker, may not work for everyone)
double_buffer yes

# Minimum size of text area
minimum_size 600 550
maximum_width 600

# Draw shades?
draw_shades no

# Draw outlines?
draw_outline no

# Draw borders around text
draw_borders no

# Stippled borders?
stippled_borders 0

# border margins
border_inner_margin 0

# Text alignment, other possible values are commented
#alignment top_left
alignment top_right
#alignment bottom_left
#alignment bottom_right

# Subtract file system buffers from used memory?
no_buffers yes

# Force UTF8? note that UTF8 support required XFT
override_utf8_locale yes

# Add spaces to keep things from moving about?  This only affects certain objects.
#use_spacer right

lua_load /home/davejr/.conky/Radar/radartest.lua
lua_draw_hook_pre radar

TEXT
```

And radartest.lua
```
--[[
call 
lua_load /home/davejr/.conky/Radar/radartest.lua
lua_draw_hook_pre radar
]]

require 'cairo'
require 'imlib2'
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
function string:split(delimiter)
local result = { }
local from  = 1
local delim_from, delim_to = string.find( self, delimiter, from  )
while delim_from do
table.insert( result, string.sub( self, from , delim_from-1 ) )
from  = delim_to + 1
delim_from, delim_to = string.find( self, delimiter, from  )
end
table.insert( result, string.sub( self, from  ) )
return result
end
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

function conky_radar()
if conky_window == nil then return end
local cs = cairo_xlib_surface_create(conky_window.display, conky_window.drawable, conky_window.visual, conky_window.width, conky_window.height)
cr = cairo_create(cs)
local updates=tonumber(conky_parse('${updates}'))
if updates>5 then
--#########################################################################################################
--#########################################################################################################
file="/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_Topo_Short.jpg"
image(300,275,0,0,file)

interval=300
timer=(updates % interval)+1
if timer==1 or updates==10 then
if timer==1 or updates==10 then
os.execute("rm /home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_N0R_0.gif")
os.execute("rm /home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_N0R_Legend_0.gif")
os.execute("rm /home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_Warnings_0.gif")

os.execute("wget --directory-prefix=/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/ http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/RadarImg/N0R/FTG_N0R_0.gif")
os.execute("wget --directory-prefix=/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/ http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/Legend/N0R/FTG_N0R_Legend_0.gif")
os.execute("wget --directory-prefix=/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/ http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/Warnings/Short/FTG_Warnings_0.gif")
end
end
file="/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_N0R_0.gif"
image(300,275,0,0,file)

file="/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_County_Short.gif"
image(300,275,0,0,file)

file="/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_Highways_Short.gif"
image(300,275,0,0,file)

file="/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_City_Short.gif"
image(300,275,0,0,file)

file="/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_N0R_Legend_0.gif"
image(300,275,0,0,file)

file="/home/davejr/.conky/Radar/FTG_Warnings_0.gif"
image(300,275,0,0,file)

end-- if updates>5
cairo_destroy(cr)
cairo_surface_destroy(cs)
cr=nil
end-- end main function

function image(center_x,center_y,w,h,file)
show = imlib_load_image(file)
if show == nil then return end
imlib_context_set_image(show)
if tonumber(w)==0 then 
width=imlib_image_get_width() 
else
width=tonumber(w)
end
if tonumber(h)==0 then 
height=imlib_image_get_height() 
else
height=tonumber(h)
end
iacross=center_x-(width/2)
idown=center_y-(height/2)
imlib_context_set_image(show)
scaled=imlib_create_cropped_scaled_image(0, 0, imlib_image_get_width(), imlib_image_get_height(), width, height)
imlib_free_image()
imlib_context_set_image(scaled)
imlib_render_image_on_drawable(iacross, idown)
imlib_free_image()
end
```

I keep all the files in one directory called 'Radar'.  Don't forget to change your location for your city and the local addressing.

Change these lines
```
cat /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages_raw| cut -c -40 > /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages
for (( i=1; i<=18; i++))
	do
		no=$(sed -n ${i}p /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages|wc -c)
		if (( no<=21 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/$/\n/" /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages
			i=$((i+1))
		elif (( no>21 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/^\(.\{21\}\)/\1\n/" /home/teo/Accuweather_Conky_USA_Images/messages
			i=$((i+1))
		fi
	done

kill -CONT $(pidof conky)
```

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-11-26
There you go:
```
cat ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages_raw| cut -c -36 > ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages
for (( i=1; i<=9; i++))
	do
		no=$(sed -n ${i}p ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages|wc -c) 
		if (( no<=13 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/$/CHLINECHLINE/" ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages
		elif (( no>13 && no<26 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/^\(.\{13\}\)/\1CHLINE/" ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages
			sed -i $i"s/$/CHLINE/" ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages
		elif (( no>26 )); then
			sed -i $i"s/^\(.\{13\}\)/\1CHLINE/" ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages
			sed -i $i"s/^\(.\{26\}\)/\1CHLINE/" ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages
		fi
	done
sed -i 's/CHLINE/\n/g' ~/.conky/Weather/Accuweather/messages

kill -CONT $(pidof conky)
```
The messages file will have 3 lines of messages now.

### Post by smilliken on 2011-11-27
> **VinDSL said:**
> Oh, okay...  :)

In **fcTemp.xslt**

```
<!-- This XSLT is used to translate an XML response from the www.google.com/ig/ XML API.

This style sheet shows all HIGH/LOW FORECAST TEMPS in the Conky Weather Section on your desktop.

Adjust the number of empty spaces (as noted below) to align the horizontal spacing of the
high/low forecast temperatures on your desktop.  This works in conjunction with the font size
that you chose to use in your .conkyrc file, and will require some patience.  :)

This is a base adjustment.  Once you get the horizontal alignment into the ballpark, the rest
of the spacing & alignment will be handled, as usual, by making adjustments to the Weather Section
in your .conkyrc file.

NOTE: Use MONO FONTS ONLY for the HIGH/LOW FORECAST TEMPS, in your .conkyrc file!

-->

<xsl:stylesheet xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform" version="1.0" >
    <xsl:output method="text" disable-output-escaping="yes" encoding="utf-8"/>
    <xsl:template match="xml_api_reply">
        <xsl:apply-templates select="weather"/>
    </xsl:template>

    <xsl:template match="weather">
        <xsl:variable name="unit_system" select="forecast_information/unit_system/@data" />
        <xsl:variable name="celsius"><xsl:text>C</xsl:text></xsl:variable>
        <xsl:variable name="fahrenheit"><xsl:text>F</xsl:text></xsl:variable>
        <xsl:for-each select="forecast_conditions[position() >= 2 ]">
            <xsl:choose>
                **[COLOR="Blue"]<xsl:when test="$unit_system != 'US'" >[/COLOR]**
                    <xsl:value-of select="round(((number(high/@data) - 32) * 5) div  9) "/> <xsl:value-of select="$celsius"/>
                    <xsl:text>/</xsl:text>
                    <xsl:value-of select="round(((number(low/@data) - 32) * 5) div  9) "/> <xsl:value-of select="$celsius"/>
                </xsl:when>
                <xsl:otherwise>
                   <xsl:value-of select="high/@data"/> <xsl:value-of select="$fahrenheit"/>
                   <xsl:text>/</xsl:text>
                   <xsl:value-of select="low/@data"/> <xsl:value-of select="$fahrenheit"/>
                </xsl:otherwise>
            </xsl:choose>
            <xsl:if test="position() != 3">
                <xsl:text>         </xsl:text><!-- Add/subtract spaces for proper Forecast Temperature alignment -->
            </xsl:if>
        </xsl:for-each>
    </xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>
```

Getting rid of the exclamation point(!) should do the trick. That's what I did.

The original script made use of a UNITS string in the bash file, but Google deprecated it.  Now it's unit_system.  

Guess I should make it a global, so it can be overridden by the bash file.

**Edit**

Or, get rid of the location altogether...  :)

${voffset 16}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -4}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${color5}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${color5}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color3}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}

${voffset -89}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=67}${color8}${alignc 99}2${font}
${voffset -64}${offset 100}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color3}/'}

   Here is everything above text in my conkyrc, you may have to edit somethings out as this is crunchbang linux setup... but I have used it on xubuntu also...the own_window_argb stuff is one thing I'am talking about. And this will put it in a window,I made my own menu items to start and stop it...

     ```
 ##############################################
#  Settings
##############################################
max_specials 10000
max_user_text 1500000
background no
use_xft yes
#xftfont Sans:size=12
#xftalpha 1
font Mono:size=8
total_run_times 0
own_window yes
own_window_title Weather Radar
own_window_argb_visual no
own_window_transparent no
own_window_type normal
own_window_hints sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
double_buffer yes
minimum_size 1000 570
maximum_width 1000
draw_shades no
draw_outline no
##############################################
draw_borders yes
border_inner_margin 5
border_outer_margin 1
border_width 5

##############################################
draw_graph_borders yes
default_color 00FFFF
default_shade_color black
default_outline_color white
alignment top_left
gap_x 30
gap_y 40
no_buffers yes
uppercase no
cpu_avg_samples 2
override_utf8_locale yes
color1 86acad #darker blue
color2 b1c9c9 #lighter blue
text_buffer_size 100000
top_name_width 10
update_interval 1
###################################################################



lua_load ~/conky/lua/radar.lua
lua_draw_hook_pre radar#starts the lua weather data gathering function, call once at top of conkyrc
######################################################################
TEXT   
```

### Post by davesbrain on 2011-12-03
Which part?
The 'mini-radar' on the left is a lua:
```
--[[
call 
lua_load /home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/radartest.lua
lua_draw_hook_pre radar
]]

require 'cairo'
require 'imlib2'
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
function string:split(delimiter)
local result = { }
local from  = 1
local delim_from, delim_to = string.find( self, delimiter, from  )
while delim_from do
table.insert( result, string.sub( self, from , delim_from-1 ) )
from  = delim_to + 1
delim_from, delim_to = string.find( self, delimiter, from  )
end
table.insert( result, string.sub( self, from  ) )
return result
end
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

function conky_radar()
if conky_window == nil then return end
local cs = cairo_xlib_surface_create(conky_window.display, conky_window.drawable, conky_window.visual, conky_window.width, conky_window.height)
cr = cairo_create(cs)
local updates=tonumber(conky_parse('${updates}'))
if updates>5 then
--#########################################################################################################
--#########################################################################################################
file="/home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/FTG_Topo_Short.jpg"
image(62,590,110,110,file)

interval=60
timer=(updates % interval)+1
if timer==1 or updates==10 then
if timer==1 or updates==10 then
os.execute("rm /home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/FTG_N0R_0.gif")
os.execute("rm /home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/FTG_N0R_Legend_0.gif")
os.execute("rm /home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/FTG_Warnings_0.gif")

os.execute("wget --directory-prefix=/home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/ http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/RadarImg/N0R/FTG_N0R_0.gif")
os.execute("wget --directory-prefix=/home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/ http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/Legend/N0R/FTG_N0R_Legend_0.gif")
os.execute("wget --directory-prefix=/home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/ http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/Warnings/Short/FTG_Warnings_0.gif")
end
end
file="/home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/FTG_N0R_0.gif"
image(62,590,110,110,file)

file="/home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/FTG_County_Short.gif"
image(62,590,110,110,file)

file="/home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/FTG_Highways_Short.gif"
image(62,590,110,110,file)

file="/home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/FTG_City_Short.gif"
image(62,590,110,110,file)

file="/home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/FTG_N0R_Legend_0.gif"
image(62,590,110,110,file)

file="/home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/FTG_Warnings_0.gif"
image(62,590,110,110,file)

end-- if updates>5
cairo_destroy(cr)
cairo_surface_destroy(cs)
cr=nil
end-- end main function

function image(center_x,center_y,w,h,file)
show = imlib_load_image(file)
if show == nil then return end
imlib_context_set_image(show)
if tonumber(w)==0 then 
width=imlib_image_get_width() 
else
width=tonumber(w)
end
if tonumber(h)==0 then 
height=imlib_image_get_height() 
else
height=tonumber(h)
end
iacross=center_x-(width/2)
idown=center_y-(height/2)
imlib_context_set_image(show)
scaled=imlib_create_cropped_scaled_image(0, 0, imlib_image_get_width(), imlib_image_get_height(), width, height)
imlib_free_image()
imlib_context_set_image(scaled)
imlib_render_image_on_drawable(iacross, idown)
imlib_free_image()
end
```

The weather part on the right is just a scaled down version of VinDSL's latest weather script.
Here is the weather part of my conky config.(wip):
```
##################
##   WEATHER    ##
##################
${voffset 2}${font Arial:bold:size=8.75}${color4}WEATHER${offset 8}${color8}${voffset -2}${hr 2}${font}
${goto 120}${font Weather:size=22}${color0}y${voffset -34}${font RadioSpace:size=19}${color3}${offset 100}${execi 1800 /home/davejr/.conky/MyConkyNew/Weather/weather.sh "Denver,CO" ctbi}${font}${goto 205}${voffset -10}${font KRARound:size=20}${color3}I${font}${goto 10}${voffset -14}${font DroidSans:size=8}${color5}${execi 1800 /home/davejr/.conky/Vin/weather.sh "Denver,CO" ctti}${font}
${goto 120}${voffset -5}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color5}${execi 1800 /home/davejr/.conky/Vin/weather.sh "Denver,CO" ccb}${font}
${goto 105}${voffset -3}${font DroidSansFallback:size=7}${color1}${execi 1800 /home/davejr/.conky/Vin/weather.sh "Denver,CO"}${font}${voffset -30}${font ConkyWeather:size=28}${color6}${goto 212}${execi 1800 /home/davejr/.conky/Vin/weather.sh "Denver,CO" cp}${font}
${voffset 8}${goto 120}${font ConkyWeather:size=25}${color6}${execi 1800 /home/davejr/.conky/Vin/weather.sh "Denver,CO" fcp}${font}
${voffset -42}${goto 130}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8}${color4}${execi 1800 /home/davejr/.conky/Vin/weather.sh "Denver,CO" dl}${font}
${voffset 13}${goto 125}${font Arial:bold:size=6.73}${color4}${execi 1800 /home/davejr/.conky/Vin/weather.sh "Denver,CO" fcti}${font}
```

### Post by davesbrain on 2011-12-03
For the radar all you need is that one lua I posted. Called 'radartest.lua', and the 'static' images for your area. 
Go here:
[http://forecast.weather.gov/jetstream/doppler/ridge_download.htm]("http://forecast.weather.gov/jetstream/doppler/ridge_download.htm")

and here:
[http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/Overlays/]("http://radar.weather.gov/ridge/Overlays/")
You will notice Long and Short...you want short.
I'm in Denver, which is 'FTG'.
Also, where it says - "image(62,590,110,110,file)" is x,y,h,w.  Position and scale.

Don't forget to add this to your conky cofig before TEXT:
```
lua_load ~/.conky/MyConkyNew/Radar/radartest.lua
lua_draw_hook_pre radar
```

As for the other weather part, I think VinDSL might be of better help. It gets complicated.

---

### Post by VinDSL on 2011-12-04
> **davesbrain said:**
> As for the other weather part, I think VinDSL might be of better help. It gets complicated.
This might help:  [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=11442872#post11442872](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=11442872#post11442872)

42dorian is pointing to the above post, in his most excellent HOWTO.

So far, it explains how to convert the script(s) from Imperial to Metric weather stats, and vice-versa.

And, I added comments to the 3-3-3 Forecast style sheets to explain how they interrelate.

### Post by 42dorian on 2011-12-05
> **wildmanne39 said:**
> Hi, I have been running VinDSL's awesome conky for the last eight months, I have tried several times to get his new weather to work but I have not been able too, can someone walk me through it or tell me which script to use to try to get TBG's weather method working in VinDSL conky?
Thank you all help is greatly appreciated.

Okay.  I've been resisting doing this.  But, truth be told... you need to be smacked in the head with it.  Once you have changed any of the code of VinDSL's conky to your own, it is no longer his.  That's the point of Conky.  It's yours.  There's nothing special about VinDSL's conky code that makes code work with it or not work with it.  It's just his code.  How he did things.  Once you make it different, it's yours.

You don't need instructions.  It's not conkyForecast anymore.  Follow the instructions on the first page.  It is already broken down step by step for you.  DON'T SKIM, READ.

---

### Post by ohnonot on 2011-12-05
> **BoredOutOfMyMind said:**
> gtk-desktop-info fed it's weather from weather.com :(  VinDSL has python scripting for Google and the api may feed into gtk-desktop-info

Any of the solutions from TBG could display a conky.  There are some with only icons....:P
i've never seen gtk-desktop-info at work so i don't really know.
my vision is that there should be a program that can display a webpage on the desktop like conky does - but afaik conky doesn't understand html.

the motivation is 
- laziness - i don't want to write a script that extracts the needed information from a webpage and then completely reformats it, like all the conky scripts do.
- also it's a finnish service, nobody on this forum is using it (with conky). also it's uncommercial and rather simple so there wouldn't be any ads popping up.
- also, why should i translate html to another language when the goals (displaying text and graphics from various sources in an optically pleasing way) are similar? well, of course there's more to the internet than just that but you get my meaning.

it's easy to get the code into conky with curl <url>, but then it just displays it as text, as a text editor would display a html document.

this is not really specific to weather forecasts but i was hoping someone here could point me in the right direction. i've done quite some googling on this and i can't find it.

apart from that, i am using one of bigusgeekus' script now, looks good and works nicely but still i have my mind set on this one particular excellent non-commercial finnish weather data provider!

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-12-06
> **BoredOutOfMyMind said:**
> source compiled and errors in gtk buttons.  I then saw revision and deleted conky_companion file.  Revision worked fine and now can see buttons. Clicking button at bottom did nothing. 

Did I miss a step?  I thought script made the TEXT.tbg file, which is missing. I did  not run the conky since no file was created/tweaked.... :confused:

Run conky_companion. 
Drag and drop elements from right to left - whatever item you want to show in the final conky.
Refine their position, align them, delete them if you want.
When ready, THEN press the Generate File button.
Read the readme.pdf in the archive for instructions.

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-12-06
> **Phyllinux said:**
> Yes I do.
I compiled the previous version of your conky_companion as I said, using gtk 2

Try changing the makefile to this
```
CC     = gcc
CFLAGS = `pkg-config --cflags --libs gtk+-2.0`

conky_companion: source/conky_companion.o source/1d1.o
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o conky_companion source/conky_companion.o source/1d1.o
	
conky_companion.o: source/conky_companion.c source/headers/1d1.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c source/conky_companion.c 
	
1d1.o: source/1d1.c source/headers/1d1.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c source/1d1.c
```
Don't touch anything in the code and make sure that the lines with $ start with a tab.

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-12-06
> **BoredOutOfMyMind said:**
> Actually, the TEXT.tbg I have renamed and attached.  As you can see from my screen print, the app added 23 lines of spacing itself in the txt file. It did not color the conky pink as I had selected, but the color of the text was changed as seen with S/R and S/S.

The next test is to see if we can use this for the other scripts. :lolflag:

The default conky colour in the app isn't there to create a conkyrc with it; it's there to help the app determine whether it should put color tags in the text lines or not; the same applies to the default conky font.

23 lines of text were added because you've left a lot of space between the beginning of the left frame (top left corner) and your first element.

I'm working on a US version at the moment; please have patience: the scripts were one thing, but this one is more difficult.

That's what your text file does for me (I've added some extra lines after the images' line, to make room for the latter).

PS: I can see from your screenshot that the images are far off. Have you fiddled with the script's output in any way?

### Post by 42dorian on 2011-12-06
Honestly TBG?  I'm on the side of abandon it.  Not that it's not a good piece of code, because it is.  I just don't like scripts that do everything for the user.  It promotes lazy code.  When something goes wrong, it gives them a middle-man program to blame instead of their own lack of understanding.  I don't like Conky-Colors for the same reason.  Some update or another renders the program invalid, and then you get a dozen people saying "It Won't WOOOOORK!  It's Broken!  You're Stupid!  Fix It For Me!"  And all they had to do was change something in the actual code themselves instead of complain about it.

It's not worth the long term hassle of promoting lazy coders.  By the looks of it, Accuweather is going to be changing it's layout often enough to take up your time with these scripts.  A task for which you have proved your worth, I'd say, about a thousand times over.  If you give new users an excuse not to learn how to do it for themselves, you're inviting future backlash.  You don't deserve that, and I think BOOMM and I would get very frustrated if we had to listen to someone bash your work out of ignorance.

But, that's me.  I suppose it's not going to be a popular point of view, but it's the one I have.  When a person does so much work on something that works so well, I don't like other people insulting it because of their own ignorance and laziness.

---

>  When a person does so much work on something that works so well, I don't like other people insulting it because of their own ignorance and laziness.

You cannot fix stupid.

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-12-07
Thanks all for the encouraging words.
Let's not forget where we are; ubuntu is a distro that is mainly addressed to people that are new to linux. So, being fresh in a new world of wonders, it'd be easier for them if things, well, just worked...
I can understand this. Although I'm all into configuring text files and do everything by myself, I can sympathize with people who just can't bother.
PCs are tools - they are used mainly for work: if a scientist or an engineer or whoever uses linux wants to have a weather forecast on their desktops, you can't force them to go through dozens of pages of documentation just to achieve this.
I feel the same about media players for example. I just want something to play some mp3s and some avi files: I don't care about db's, playlists etc. I just want the damn thing to work without much fuss.
So, to conclude, I'll answer every seemingly stupid question in this thread - as long as it's not trolling, which I like and do it myself but I hate when others do it to me :mrgreen:

### Post by 42dorian on 2011-12-07
> **TeoBigusGeekus said:**
> Thanks all for the encouraging words.
Let's not forget where we are; ubuntu is a distro that is mainly addressed to people that are new to linux. So, being fresh in a new world of wonders, it'd be easier for them if things, well, just worked...
I can understand this. Although I'm all into configuring text files and do everything by myself, I can sympathize with people who just can't bother.
PCs are tools - they are used mainly for work: if a scientist or an engineer or whoever uses linux wants to have a weather forecast on their desktops, you can't force them to go through dozens of pages of documentation just to achieve this.
I feel the same about media players for example. I just want something to play some mp3s and some avi files: I don't care about db's, playlists etc. I just want the damn thing to work without much fuss.
So, to conclude, I'll answer every seemingly stupid question in this thread - as long as it's not trolling, which I like and do it myself but I hate when others do it to me :mrgreen:


Here's the thing TBG.  Conky and your scripts both may have huge threads devoted to them at this point, but they are far from complex.  All it takes is one really well documented source and it satisfies both new users and old pros.  Laziness is looking at the thing, not having touched it, and wanting it to "Just Work" they way you want it to.  Even an MP3 player needs you to hit "Play" to make it play.  Conky is no different.  It's only when you assume it's too complicated that you have problems.  Once you dive in, it's easy.  For new people and pros alike.

By the time they've got your little script shortcut running, they also have to know where their Conky script is, how to put code in, how to test it, how to download and test fonts, how to kill and restart Conky, and generally know how to use spacing.  That's just how Conky is.  Once you're past the built in commands and into scripts of any sort, you've already got to have some knowledge under your belt.  And by the time you've got some knowledge under your belt, the shortcuts are obsolete to you anyways.  It's when you use the shortcut without knowing what you're doing that you end up with people coming back mad that something "Broke" on them.

I've seen it more often than I'd like to.  Because Conky is so simple to get running for yourself, people assume that these complex setups are difficult to learn.  So they take a shortcut.  They don't know how to use it, so they assume it's broken.  What's broken is them.  They didn't take the time to learn first.  Conky, ever since VinDSL got popular with his setups, has become counter-intuitive.  It looks like you can just jump in and it'll work like magic, but it doesn't.  It doesn't work like magic until after you've learned the basics.  By the time you learn the basics enough to get these scripts working, I would say a few hours of reading, you no longer need a shortcut because it's easier to do yourself.

If you give users an excuse to be lazy, they will come back having failed, and blame you.  Instead, I say promote a well documented source of information on your scripts, and point them there as a first step to getting it running.  They need to know the basics anyways, so they might as well learn it.  Conky isn't magic, neither is Linux.  They will learn in the course of getting it running, so there is no point in doing anything other than giving them something to read that will get them running.  That's all the shortcut anyone needs.  It's easy after that.

---

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-12-07
@42dorian
You can't be hyper active about everything: some users specialize in security, others in programming, others in scripting, others in conky, etc.
Imagine this: you want conky to work, but you just don't have enough time, or ever worse mind clarity, to busy yourself with one more thing that requires your attention.
Linus Torvalds could say that kernel programming is not that difficult; everyone should learn C and Assembly and try their hands in creating one - why should I bother spoon-feeding them? 
I admit that the example above is a bit extreme, but extreme examples make things clearer. At least this is how I see it...

---

### Post by 42dorian on 2011-12-07
If you don't have the clarity of mind or patience to learn Conky basics before you start, then you won't be able to fix what's wrong when there's an update.  And there are updates and changes to all the different components.  For the same reason you actually take some lessons on how to drive before you make the attempt, taking the time to get everything together before you start Conky ensures it actually works.

We're talking a couple hours, maximum, for Conky.  Conky basics are just a foundation for the whole thing.  Once you have the basics, just the simple tips and tricks like getting fonts, reloading, the use of actually calling and restarting Conky, and spacing/font sizes, then the rest is easy.  It just falls into place every time.  And by the time you know these things, only a couple hours have passed, and these shortcuts are pointless.  You can do it faster yourself and locate where everything is easier than running the separate shortcut.  If anything, the shortcuts take more effort and patience to get them right than just doing it yourself.  It's counter-intuitive, but that's the way the results are.

Copying someone else's code and learning what spacing, fonts, and scripts do through changing things to suit the screen differences takes less time, effort, and concentration than running a script that does everything for you.  And in the process of doing it yourself, you know exactly what breaks and where to fix it when there's an update.

By the way, I just type fast, that's why there's so much to read.  I just happen to type fast enough that I try to use proper English instead of text speak.  Fast fingers affords me more time to get things right when typing, that's all.

---

### Post by TeoBigusGeekus on 2011-12-07
> **42dorian said:**
> If you don't have the clarity of mind or patience to learn Conky basics before you start, then you won't be able to fix what's wrong when there's an update.  And there are updates and changes to all the different components.  For the same reason you actually take some lessons on how to drive before you make the attempt, taking the time to get everything together before you start Conky ensures it actually works.

We're talking a couple hours, maximum, for Conky.  Conky basics are just a foundation for the whole thing.  Once you have the basics, just the simple tips and tricks like getting fonts, reloading, the use of actually calling and restarting Conky, and spacing/font sizes, then the rest is easy.  It just falls into place every time.  And by the time you know these things, only a couple hours have passed, and these shortcuts are pointless.  You can do it faster yourself and locate where everything is easier than running the separate shortcut.  If anything, the shortcuts take more effort and patience to get them right than just doing it yourself.  It's counter-intuitive, but that's the way the results are.

Copying someone else's code and learning what spacing, fonts, and scripts do through changing things to suit the screen differences takes less time, effort, and concentration than running a script that does everything for you.  And in the process of doing it yourself, you know exactly what breaks and where to fix it when there's an update.

By the way, I just type fast, that's why there's so much to read.  I just happen to type fast enough that I try to use proper English instead of text speak.  Fast fingers affords me more time to get things right when typing, that's all.

In principle, I agree.
But there are people who just don't give a damn; either it works with minimum effort or it doesn't.

### Post by VastOne on 2011-12-07

### Post by 42dorian on 2011-12-07
Yeah.  I agree about them wanting it to be of minimal effort or not at all.  The weird part is, in Conky, the reality is that by the time you get it set up by learning the basics first, you no longer need the "Shortcut" program.  The actual easy way to do it is to learn basics first.  Not the entire code, just basics.  A couple hours training yourself and you're set for life.

Flash in Fedora is about Flash having bugs in it that get fixed, and some users wanting to get rid of it all together.  But, we've probably all seen where one update to some element or another (Accuweather for example, though just an example and not the point) happens to the user and what they had stops working.  No bug, just an update that renders what they've got before useless.  It can't "Just Work" because it wasn't the program, the user, or Conky that changed things.  Understanding the basics first, you come back and check for updated scripts.  The "Lazy Way" as I keep saying is knowing nothing, coming back, and chewing the author out for bad code.  I hate that.  You did a lot of work, and you don't deserve that kind of vitriol.  If they just used the scripts without the shortcut, they wouldn't have anything to claim as "Broken" in the first place.

Updates break Conky setups all the time, with easy fixes.  If you want everything done for you, Conky is the last place a user should go to.  It's actually more effort and time out of your life to have other people and shortcut programs do it for you.  Not from the standpoint of a coder, just from the standpoint of actual Conky code.  You can, much easier, copy and paste code that isn't working to one of these threads and copy and paste someone's solution back, than you can run a script or program that needs updating before it'll work with new changes.

And, yes, VastOne.  I type fast.  I won't slow down just because proper English and full sentence structure is perceived as angry or aggressive on a forum when no such anger or aggression is actually in said text.  I find it far more difficult to have to abbreviate thoughts that can later be misinterpreted as something else entirely.

---
